2. Prompt assembly uses the helper at every artifact-body interpolation site in scope.
3. `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` proves the delimiter contract and payload preservation.

description:
PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.

description:
PROBLEM
Operators must be able to modify bead queues for any project from the web UI, including spoke-owned projects when using a hub.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:12 returns a bead store rooted only at the per-request working directory.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:68 creates beads through that local store.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:112 updates beads through that local store.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:353 requires bead queue mutations to be project-scoped and owner-targeted in hub-mode.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340 defines TC-018 for cross-project bead queue mutations, including federated writes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route beadCreate, beadUpdate, beadApprove, beadBlock, beadCancel, beadReopen, and dependency mutations when present through local project context or federation owner forwarding.
- Preserve local project isolation for standalone and same-node targets.
- Preserve lifecycle events and include origin/forwarding audit metadata for forwarded writes.
- Return typed errors for stale state, missing required reasons/notes, non-owned targets, and offline/read_only spokes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add new lifecycle states or dependency-edit UI beyond already exposed mutation surfaces.
title: server: route bead queue mutations through federation owner forwarding

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
`ddx doctor` must surface both install tiers so operators can see whether a plugin is installed globally, per-project, or lazy-resolving to global. The governing spec/docs must also document the dual install topology and lookup precedence so the behavior is discoverable and durable.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:418 `reportSkillInstallTopology(projectRoot)` computes a global path and a project path with statuses (doctor.go:436/441/444 `lazy-resolves-to-global`) and prints two lines at doctor.go:447-448: `Global Install (%s) — %s` and `Project Install (%s) — %s`. These lines lack a regression test.
- The dual-topology decision and precedence are not yet fully reflected in the spec/docs: ADR-027 (skill-install-topology), library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works", and CLAUDE.md "Persona System".

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a test asserting `ddx doctor` output contains both the `Global Install (` and `Project Install (` lines with a correct status (`ok` | `missing` | `lazy-resolves-to-global`) given seeded/absent tiers.
- Update docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md to document install-to-agent (global) vs install-in-project (in-tree + convention) and the project→global→baked-in precedence.
- Update CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section to state the install precedence, and library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" to document install locations. Land all spec/doc edits in this bead's commit.

NON-SCOPE
- Install/init/update behavioral changes — sibling beads (this bead consumes their behavior for the doctor report only).
- Removing the per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Sibling install/update beads (doctor reports the layers they create); land this bead after they merge so the report reflects real behavior.
title: doctor + docs: report both install layers and land skill-install-topology spec updates

description:
PROBLEM
DDx has no runtime concept of a "global" plugin install nor a precedence order for resolving a plugin name. Every install/lookup path is hardcoded to the per-project tree via ddxroot.Path(). Before `--global` install (sibling bead) or `ddx init --global` (sibling bead) can exist, there must be a single resolver that, given a plugin name, returns its on-disk path by checking the project layer first, then a global layer, then the baked-in default.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/installer.go:66 `InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, src, projectRoot)` only knows the project root; there is no global target.
- cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75 `Path()` resolves only the project root (in-tree `<project>/.ddx` or convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` at line 86). There is no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` helper.
- cli/internal/skills/install.go:28 `Install(src, projectRoot, opts)` and all callers assume a single project destination.
- No `ResolvePlugin`-style function exists anywhere under cli/internal/registry.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `GlobalDir()` to cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go returning `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` (reuse the existing `xdgDataHome()` helper at ddxroot.go:89). The plugins root is `GlobalDir()/plugins`.
2. Add `registry.ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name string) (path string, layer string, error)` implementing precedence:
   - `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name>` if the dir exists → layer "project".
   - `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>` if it exists → layer "global".
   - baked-in default (defaultplugin.FS, the `ddx` plugin only) → layer "baked-in"; return an error for any other unresolved name.
3. Export the layer string so doctor/init/install (sibling beads) can report which layer answered.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring `--global` into install/update commands (sibling bead: install/update --global).
- `ddx init --global` and agent-tier symlinks (sibling bead: init topology).
- `ddx doctor` reporting (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Removing the existing per-project install. Both layers coexist.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ddxroot.Path()` and the new `GlobalDir()` must share the same XDG_DATA_HOME convention agreed there. Sibling beads in this set depend on this resolver.
title: registry: add two-layer plugin resolution (project → global → baked-in) with global XDG path helper

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/bead/store.go Close() performs a one-hop supersession cascade post-close, gated by every scope check listed in the description.
2. TestClose_SupersededCascade_Eligible in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go: creates Y (open), X (open, superseded-by:Y, attempt_count==0, no other state), closes Y, asserts X is closed with a superseded_close event whose body cites closed_by_cascade_of: Y.
3. TestClose_SupersededCascade_OperatorNotedNotCascaded: creates Y, X (open, superseded-by:Y, has operator notes added after the supersession marker timestamp), closes Y, asserts X remains open and no superseded_close event was emitted.
4. TestClose_SupersededCascade_OneHopCycleGuard: creates Y, X (open, superseded-by:Y), Z (open, superseded-by:X). Closes Y. Asserts X closes, Z remains open (cascade deferred), no infinite loop.
5. TestClose_SupersededCascade_Idempotent: closing an already-closed Y a second time produces no new events and no mutations.
6. TestClose_SupersededCascade_ClaimedOrInProgress_NotCascaded: X has a live claim_id; closing Y does not cascade X.

description:
PROBLEM
Beads marked superseded-by:<Y> never close automatically when Y closes. ddx-847df529 (parent ddx-09d7d787) carries superseded-by:ddx-c2a66f45-wt-6bee7238 and a note "Do not dispatch this bead independently"; the superseder closed weeks ago but the leftover stays open and blocks its parent epic. This is a primary contributor to the stuck-queue state on the current project (RC-2 in the FEAT-010 reason taxonomy).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go Close(Y) closes only Y. It does not look for open beads X with superseded-by==Y and cascade-close them. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires this cascade with a strict scope guard.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend cli/internal/bead/store.go Close(Y) with a post-close cascade step. After Y closes, scan for open beads X where supersession-by-Y is the sole remaining reason to stay open. Strict scope contract (each must hold; if any fails, surface in ddx work focus Section A, do NOT cascade):
- X is open AND (X.superseded-by == Y OR X carries label superseded-by:Y) AND Y is now closed.
- X has no other open dependents (no open bead Z with Z.dependencies referencing X).
- X has no open dependencies of its own beyond the supersession marker.
- X has attempt_count == 0.
- X has no operator notes added after the supersession marker timestamp.
- X is not currently claimed or in-progress (no live claim_id).
- X carries no manual-hold / no-auto-close / container override label.

Re-read X's current tracker state inside the same Close(Y) invocation before mutating — a concurrent worker may have changed X since the scan started.

Idempotent: re-running Close(Y) is a no-op for already-closed X.
Cycle-guarded: track visited IDs within one Close(Y) call; cascade is one hop deep. If X is a superseder for an open Z, Z's cascade is DEFERRED to a separate operator action (do not transitively cascade).
Audit: emit a superseded_close event on X with body {closed_by_cascade_of: Y, reason: "closed_as_superseded_via:Y"}. Operators can grep events to reverse.

Add operator override flag at the work command surface in a separate bead (--no-auto-supersede-close); the Close() code path always runs the cascade unless the flag has been threaded through. For this bead, accept the cascade always firing; the flag wiring is the work-loop bead's responsibility.

NON-SCOPE
- Cascading transitively beyond one hop (deferred to a future bead if ever needed).
- The work-loop wiring of --no-auto-supersede-close flag (separate bead).
- RC-3 dead-intermediate cleanup walk-up (separate bead, depends on this one).

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. No deps.
title: store: cascade-close superseded children inside Close()

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)
- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)
- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/
- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)

A regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.

SPEC IMPACT — the following docs reference .ddx/ paths and must be updated in this bead:
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md (state paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021-operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path.md (operator-prompt paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md (worker status paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md (FEAT-010 runs/)

description:
PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.
title: C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps + pick() derives from drain.result

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx install` and `ddx update` only ever target the per-project plugin tree. There is no way to install a skill/persona/plugin once per machine, so operators must repeat installs in every project. Three install destinations must be supported: global (XDG), project in-tree (`<project>/.ddx`), and project convention (XDG projects/...).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go:36 `RunE: f.runInstall` and its flag set (install.go:38-39) have no `--global` flag; install always targets the project under `.ddx/plugins/<name>/` (install.go:27-28).
- cli/cmd/update.go:57 `runUpdate` and update.go:194 `performUpdate` operate only on the working dir's project tree; update.go:112 `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets(workingDir)` is project-scoped.
- Both route through ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and never reference the global layer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx install` (cli/cmd/install.go) and `ddx update` (cli/cmd/update.go).
2. install --global: write content to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` (via `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from the resolver bead) and create symlinks/copies into agent-tier paths `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>` (copy on platforms lacking symlink support).
3. install (no --global): keep writing to `ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/` and link into `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` AND `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`. In in-tree mode the target is `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>`; in convention mode it is `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>` and the project agent-tier links point into that XDG path.
4. update --global updates only the global plugin tree; update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode (in-tree vs convention) is active, via `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets`.

NON-SCOPE
- The plugin resolver/precedence (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution) — consume `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from it.
- `ddx init --global` first-time setup (sibling bead: init topology).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Removing per-project install; both modes coexist.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution); sibling registry-resolver bead (provides `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` and layer semantics).

description:
PROBLEM
  Story 14 requires federated query resolvers that aggregate beads, runs, and projects across registered spokes. Today the GraphQL schema at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql defines FederatedBead, FederatedRun, and FederatedProject types and the corresponding query fields, but write-routing (hub forwards mutations to owning spoke) is not implemented, and the resolver_federation.go file has no mutation forwarding.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:61 defines FederatedBeads, :124 defines FederatedRuns, and :184 defines FederatedProjects — all read-only fan-out via FederationProvider.FanOut (cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:31). cli/internal/federation/fanout.go:208 provides the FanOutClient.Execute fan-out transport. No mutation resolver in resolver_mutation_*.go calls FanOut or routes writes to an owning spoke; mutations remain hub-local. The ownerSpoke routing metadata fields (nodeID, spokeURL) are populated by existing fan-out code but no write-routing contract enforces "hub forwards, never broadcasts."

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add write-routing helper in cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go: routeToOwner(ctx, ownerSpokeURL, mutationQuery, variables) error that forwards mutations via HTTP to the owning spoke.
  - Wire routeToOwner into the relevant mutation resolvers in resolver_mutation_beads.go or resolver_mutation_runs.go for any mutation that operates on a federated resource.
  - Add schema.graphql comment block documenting the write-routing contract ("hub forwards to ownerSpoke; never broadcasts").
  - Name the ownerSpoke field on FederatedBead/FederatedRun/FederatedProject as the routing discriminator.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery and registration (covered by ddx-fe63969f).
  - Federated mutations on FederatedProject (out of Story 14 scope; read-only for projects).
title: federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields

description:
CRITICAL FINDING from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md: code added by recently-landed beads has tests but NO production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. The drain has been closing beads as success while the actual fix code sits unwired. This explains why operational pain (worker auto-exit, lock contention) kept recurring even after fixes 'landed.'

Affected functions (per audit):
- escalation.Ladder.Next (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go) — added by S10 ladder work, no caller
- EvaluateRetryPolicy (cli/internal/agent/retry_policy.go) — added by recent retry work, no caller
- RunWithLockBackoff (cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go or similar) — added by ddx-da11a34a lock contention, no caller
- DispatchPostAttempt — added by ddx-aede917d or sibling, no caller

For each: decide wire vs delete based on whether the BEAD that added it is still relevant (its acceptance criteria still describes desired behavior) OR whether subsequent design changes have made it obsolete.

Recommended decision: **WIRE all four**. The beads that added them are recently-landed and the acceptance criteria are still relevant. The unwired state is a process failure (the implementer wrote and tested the code but didn't replace the old code path). Wiring is the right fix; delete only if the underlying behavior is genuinely obsolete (none of these are).

After wiring, verify with: drain runs and observes the new code paths firing in execution evidence. Spot-check 3-5 beads' .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json for evidence of escalation/retry/triage events.
notes: superseded by REACH-BACKFILL (ddx-83440482); the 4 unwired functions (escalation.Ladder.Next, EvaluateRetryPolicy, RunWithLockBackoff, DispatchPostAttempt) surfaced as initial production-reachability violations and are absorbed into child bead ddx-83b8994f (internal/agent backfill).
title: audit + wire (or delete) the unwired escalation/retry/triage code from recent landed beads

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.

# PROPOSED FIX

Per the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):

1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:
   ```go
   type Migrator interface {
       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error
       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP
       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)
       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)
   }

   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)
   ```
   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.

2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:
   ```go
   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})
   if err != nil { ... }
   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)
   ```

4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.

5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.

# NON-SCOPE
- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.
- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.
- Other concerns — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.
2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.
3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.
4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.
5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.
6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:migration, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

description:
PROBLEM: execute-bead workers hold git locks across the entire LLM subprocess invocation, blocking concurrent tracker operations.

FIX: Refactor to acquire locks only for the duration of git mutations, not across subprocess waits. Specifically:
- Scope .git/index.lock acquisition to actual git commands
- Scope .ddx/.git-tracker.lock acquisition to tracker file writes
- Never hold locks while waiting on claude/codex/gemini subprocess

CROSS-REF: Parent ddx-57c40485; original Fizeau incident AR-2026-05-17-repo.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing)
- cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go (if needed)
- Test: git_index_lock_test.go
title: Fix lock scoping: release .git/index.lock and .ddx/.git-tracker.lock before harness subprocess

description:
PROBLEM
  The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick. There is no injection seam, so tests cannot stub the ticker to verify heartbeat cadence or cancellation without real wall-clock delays.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extract to withHeartbeat(ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store interface{ Heartbeat(string) error }, fn func() (ExecuteBeadReport, error)) error in a new file cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go.
  - withHeartbeat starts the internal goroutine + WaitGroup, calls fn(), cancels the ticker goroutine on fn return, and returns fn's result.
  - Drain loop body (execute_bead_loop.go:732) replaces the inline goroutine block with a single withHeartbeat call.
  - Add TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn and TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates to cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go using a stub ticker channel injection.

NON-SCOPE
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Changes to heartbeatInterval policy or the Store.Heartbeat call itself.
  - Any behavior change to the drain loop; this is a pure extraction.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: heartbeat timing and lifecycle belong in one helper, not inline with candidate execution.
- P4: one bead heartbeat bug must not leak into the rest of the drain loop.
- P5: extracted heartbeat behavior supports always-visible worker state.

description:
PROBLEM
  The run/try/work dispatch paths must not set Profile, Harness, or Model directly on ResolvedConfig or Runtime structs — those are durable knobs that belong in config.LoadAndResolve. Without a mechanically-enforced lint rule, future edits can silently reintroduce the anti-pattern and bypass the fizeau routing contract.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go (package routinglint) currently forbids retired compensating-routing identifiers (ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef, etc.) and flag literals (--escalate, profile_ladders). It does not yet forbid direct field assignment of Profile/Harness/Model on Runtime structs inside run/try/work command code paths. The SD-024 runtimelint analyzer (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go:41) forbids durable-knob fields on *Runtime structs generically, but does not name a per-command allowlist or produce a test that verifies the run/work/try paths specifically. No TestRoutingLint* test exercises this invariant.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extend routinglint (cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go) to flag direct Profile/Harness/Model field assignment on types matching *Runtime in cli/cmd/ run/work/try command files, or add a new rule in runtimelint covering this.
  - Alternatively, add a targeted integration test TestRoutingLint_RunPathNeverSetsProfileDirectly that runs the analyzer against the live tree and asserts zero diagnostics for the assignment pattern.
  - Fix any existing violations: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1893-1897 passes overrides.Profile/Harness/Model through CLIOverrides to config.LoadAndResolve — verify this is compliant (CLIOverrides is not a Runtime struct).

NON-SCOPE
  - Changes to agent_run or agent_run_overrides command behavior.
  - Changes to the fizeau or ladder routing logic.
  - Lint rules for non-routing fields on Runtime structs (covered by runtimelint).

acceptance:
1. Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
2. 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable. Test: ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge.
3. ddx work no-progress remediation: when an attempted bead's outcome is 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale' AND any freshly-created child carries the attempted bead in its deps, ddx auto-emits an 'operator_attention' event with reason 'parent_back_edge' and stops re-claiming that bead. (Today it loops indefinitely.) Test: ddx_work_halts_on_parent_back_edge.
4. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable. Test: ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge.
- ddx work no-progress remediation: when an attempted bead's outcome is 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale' AND any freshly-created child carries the attempted bead in its deps, ddx auto-emits an 'operator_attention' event with reason 'parent_back_edge' and stops re-claiming that bead. (Today it loops indefinitely.) Test: ddx_work_halts_on_parent_back_edge.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. `ddx init --global --silent` exits 0 and creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ with the bundled library content.
2. `ddx init --global` is idempotent: running twice does not error and does not destroy local-only files under global/.
3. TestInitGlobal_PopulatesXDGGlobalDir asserts the directory structure post-install matches what convention mode expects.
4. TestInitGlobal_IsIdempotent asserts a second run does not error and re-syncs the plugin content.
5. TestConventionMode_ReadsGlobalSkillsBeforeHardcoded asserts that with --global installed, convention mode picks up the global plugin instead of the hardcoded one.
6. ddx doctor's "Global Install" check exists and reports the install path + status.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/config/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Convention mode (per ddx-ad338ec3) bootstraps an XDG project dir but has no source for skills, personas, or the default ddx plugin. Either every project re-downloads the plugin on first use, or the user must have a global install. The latter is the better answer: install once per machine, all convention-mode projects share.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no per-user install path today. `ddx init` only writes per-project state. Skills/personas live in the project's .ddx/plugins/ (in-tree) or are missing entirely (convention).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `ddx init --global` flag (and corresponding code path in cli/cmd/init.go). When set:
   - Resolve target path: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/).
   - Install the default ddx plugin (library/ contents) into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
   - Write a default ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (library URL, branch, etc.).
   - Idempotent: re-running upgrades to a newer plugin version but does not destroy operator state.

2. Convention mode reads ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ as the second precedence tier (in-tree > global > hardcoded). If --global has never been run, fall back to baked-in hardcoded defaults — convention mode still works without it; --global is an optimization for users who want offline-cacheable plugin content.

3. Document the install path in ddx doctor: a new check "Global Install" reports whether ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ exists and points operators to `ddx init --global` if it does not.

4. Do NOT remove the existing per-project plugin install (in-tree mode at .ddx/plugins/<name>/). Both coexist.

NON-SCOPE
- In-project ddx init manifest write (E4 bead).
- Migration from legacy ~/.config/ddx/ or ~/.ddx/ if such exist on disk (none expected; today there is no global install).
- Plugin update / upgrade flow for the global install (use existing `ddx update` mechanism; verify it works against the new path).

DEPS
ddx-564ea1cf (E1) — resolver must exist.
ddx-ad338ec3 (E2) — convention mode reads from this path.
notes: Superseded by epic restructure into 3 consolidated beads aligned to the two principles: (1) bulletproof ddx home resolution, (2) centralized vs in-project skills/personas. New beads pending.
title: config: ddx init --global installs user-tier skills/personas to XDG global

description:
PROBLEM
Adding cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ (from ddx-1a9cc01f) creates a new production entry package. The REACH-PROTO reachability checker (ddx-a946c744) uses a list of entry roots to determine which code is reachable. Without updating that root list, the new executeloop package will be incorrectly flagged as unreachable dead code.

ROOT CAUSE
- The REACH-PROTO tool (ddx-a946c744) maintains an explicit list of entry packages in its root configuration. Currently cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ does not exist; once ddx-1a9cc01f lands, it will be a production package reachable from cli/cmd/ but the reachability root list will not reflect this until this bead updates it.
- Parent note: ddx-29058e2a §REACHABILITY-PROTO explicitly calls out that REACH-PROTO must know about cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ as an entry root.

PROPOSED FIX
- After ddx-1a9cc01f lands: locate the REACH-PROTO root configuration (likely under cli/internal/reachability/ or in the REACH-PROTO tool referenced by ddx-a946c744).
- Add cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ as an entry root.
- Verify: reachability checker reports executeloop package as reachable (not dead code).

NON-SCOPE
- Reachability analysis of other packages added by later children (each child handles its own roots).
- Changes to the executeloop package itself.
title: agent: update reachability roots for ExecuteLoopSpec package

acceptance:
1. Go test `TestFederatedStartWorker_ForwardsToOwner` proves hub startWorker for a spoke project forwards to the spoke and does not dispatch locally.
2. Go test `TestFederatedStopWorker_ForwardsToOwner` proves hub stopWorker for a spoke worker forwards to the owning spoke.
3. Go test `TestFederatedStartWorker_RejectsOfflineSpoke` proves offline/read_only targets return operator-visible errors and create no local worker rows.
4. Playwright `federation-worker-control.spec.ts` includes tests named `starts a spoke worker from the hub`, `stops a spoke worker from the hub`, and `refuses commands for an offline spoke`.
5. `cd cli && go test -short ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/...` passes.
6. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/federation-worker-control.spec.ts` passes once UI wiring lands.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The hub worker UI must be able to start and stop workers for spoke-owned projects, but the current GraphQL worker mutations execute only against the local resolver action dispatcher.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:88 resolves StartWorker locally and cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:138 calls `r.Actions.DispatchWorker` against `projectRoot`.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:142 resolves StopWorker locally and cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:146 calls `r.Actions.StopWorker` directly.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:350 requires hub-mode worker start/stop to be owner-targeted and refused for offline or non-writable spokes.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:334 through docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:338 require federated worker list, start, stop, offline refusal, and idempotency.

PROPOSED FIX
- Teach StartWorker and StopWorker resolvers to detect non-local owner targets and route through the federation forwarding interface.
- Preserve local behavior for standalone and local-node targets.
- Ensure remote worker starts use count/cap/idempotency behavior from the hardened StartWorker contract.
- Ensure remote stops target the node that owns the worker id and surface terminal state back to the hub UI.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement bead or document forwarding in this bead. Do not implement FEAT-029 managed-node outbound control.
title: server: route worker start and stop through federation owner forwarding

acceptance:
1. registry.GlobalPluginsDir() returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins, honoring an XDG_DATA_HOME override and falling back to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/plugins.
2. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry): with <name> present in BOTH ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name> and GlobalPluginsDir()/<name>, ResolvePluginDir returns the project dir and scope 'project'.
3. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry): with the project copy absent and the global copy present, ResolvePluginDir returns the global dir and scope 'global'.
4. Existing FEAT-015 home-target rejection tests in cli/internal/registry remain green (no weakening of the ~ guard).
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/registry/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (foundation child of ddx-937de9fc)
Plugins resolve only to project-local paths today. internal/registry has no concept of a machine-global plugin tree (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>) nor a precedence order for runtime lookup. This child lands the path resolver and precedence that the --global install/update/init/doctor children all build on.

ROOT CAUSE
- No resolver maps (projectRoot, name) -> a concrete plugin dir with precedence.
- cli/internal/registry/registry.go exposes BuiltinRegistry/Find only; there is no GlobalPluginsDir / ResolvePluginDir.
- The XDG data-home helper xdgDataHome() in cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:89 is unexported; project root comes from ddxroot.Path() (ddxroot.go:75).
- cli/internal/registry/installer.go rejects any home-rooted (~) install target at installer.go:43, :79, :135 (FEAT-015). That guard must stay for project-scope installs; this child does NOT write any global tree, only resolves paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add registry.GlobalPluginsDir() string returning filepath.Join(<xdg-data-home>, 'ddx', 'global', 'plugins'), honoring XDG_DATA_HOME and the ~/.local/share fallback (mirror ddxroot.xdgDataHome semantics; export a shared helper rather than duplicating).
2. Add registry.ResolvePluginDir(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, name string) (dir, scope string, ok bool) with precedence:
   a. ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name> if it exists -> scope 'project'.
   b. GlobalPluginsDir()/<name> if it exists -> scope 'global'.
   c. baked-in default plugin (only name=='ddx', via internal/registry/defaultplugin) -> scope 'baked-in'.
   d. otherwise ok=false.
3. Leave FEAT-015 ~-target rejection in installer.go untouched.

NON-SCOPE: wiring --global into install/update/init/doctor (sibling children); writing/symlinking the global tree.

DEPS: none new (parent dep ddx-06cbaa90 closed; ddxroot.Path already lands).
title: registry: global plugins dir resolution + project→global→baked-in plugin lookup precedence

description:
PROBLEM
Execute-loop execution fields are duplicated across cobra extraction, REST/GraphQL request structs, server ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec persistence, and runWorker consumption. Adding a worker mode or idle interval can silently drop it on one transport.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 defines ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec as a server-local struct while cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts a wider flag set, cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 has another request struct, and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 has a third. The type boundary is not shared, and the old polling field name conflicts with the desired public semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small import-safe package under cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ that defines ExecuteLoopSpec with JSON tags and fields ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, and SpecVersion.
- Define Mode values for once, drain, and watch. CLI parsing may keep --once, but the persisted/API spec uses Mode rather than a boolean once field.
- IdleInterval is the sleep duration used only when Mode=watch and the queue has no execution-ready work.
- Do not add or preserve a PollInterval field, poll_interval JSON field, or public compatibility alias in the new spec.
- Add DispatchOptions with only Local and JSON control-plane fields; do not persist it.
- Add Duration JSON support that marshals as strings and unmarshals both strings and numeric nanoseconds.
- Add ApplyDefaults and Validate methods, including SpecVersion support and a code comment documenting the single-version client/server forward-compat stance.
- Do not wire cobra, REST, GraphQL, or runWorker callers in this bead; later children consume this package.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change LabelFilter from string.
- Do not change work validation policy beyond moving existing checks behind the spec lifecycle.
- Do not modify server or cobra call sites except imports needed by tests.
notes: Unblocked after fresh-eyes cleanup removed stale PollInterval/poll_interval compatibility-alias requirements. The bead is now a standalone canonical ExecuteLoopSpec package slice with Mode + IdleInterval semantics and no unresolved investigation item.
title: agent: introduce canonical ExecuteLoopSpec package

The command is now 'ddx work' (with ddx agent work as a back-compat alias), and the FEAT-010 refactor (ddx-5cb6e6cd) moves to a work package. ROOT CAUSE

- C13 (ddx-387a0178) plans 'file/identifier rename per refactor proposal §3'. If C13 only renames identifiers (not the file), this bead handles the file rename. If C13 subsumes the file rename, this bead should be closed as duplicate — verify after C13 lands.

acceptance:
1. `TestInstallGlobal_WritesGlobalTreeAndLinksAgentTier` (cli/cmd/install_global_test.go:184) asserts `ddx install <name> --global --silent` writes to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` and creates symlink-or-copy entries under `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>`, while leaving the project `.claude/skills` and `.agents/skills` untouched.
2. `TestInstallGlobal_WritesXDGAndAgentTierLinks` (install_global_test.go:48) asserts the same XDG + agent-tier link layout for AC1.
3. `TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject` (cli/cmd/update_test.go:211) asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and leaves the project install untouched.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a, slice 2 of 4)

Operators who want one Claude Code / Codex install per machine need a `--global` surface so a plugin is written once to the machine-wide tree and linked into the home-level harness skill directories, available across every project without per-project setup.

ROOT CAUSE
- `runInstall` (cli/cmd/install.go:54) reads the `--global` flag (install.go:49,57) and routes target resolution through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184). When global is true it sets `installRoot = ddxroot.GlobalDir()` and skill dirs to `~/.agents/skills` and `~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-192); state file selection is via `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202-215). Local installs route the same way in `installLocal` (install.go:358, global branch install.go:450-507).
- Update: `performGlobalUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:300) operates exclusively on `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/` via `registry.InstallPackage(adjustedPkg, ddxroot.GlobalDir())` (update.go:337) and saves the global state (update.go:346); the `--global` flag is parsed in `extractUpdateOptions` (update.go:395) into `UpdateOptions.Global` (update.go:35) and dispatched from `runUpdate` (update.go:65).

PROPOSED FIX
Ensure `ddx install <name> --global` writes the plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` and creates symlink-or-copy entries under `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>`, and does NOT touch the project's `.claude/skills` / `.agents/skills`. Ensure `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and global state, never the project tree. Expose `--global` consistently on the plugin query surface (`ddx plugin list` / `installed`).

NON-SCOPE
- The no-flag project install in either mode (slice 3).
- `ddx init --global` first-time setup and doctor reporting (slice 4).
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- Slice 1 (plugin lookup precedence) — global installs are resolved via `ResolvePlugin`'s global layer.
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — depends on `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.
title: global install surface: ddx install --global and ddx update --global write the machine-wide plugin tree + agent-tier links

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-eea2dfca is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead only adds the Niflheim observation as regression coverage and decision/audit wiring.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_PreClaimDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, pre-claim fingerprint/threshold facts, and operator-attention reason for the repeated warning family.
- Existing related behavior from `ddx-d67f124e` remains covered by reuse/alignment with its helpers or by waiting on that dependency; this bead does not add a duplicate pre-claim warning escalation implementation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
The server operator workbench now has explicit user stories and test-plan rows, but there are no focused E2E specs proving the basic worker single-pane and server-managed launch workflows.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:485 defines US-100 for seeing every worker in one pane.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:510 defines US-101 for starting server-managed ddx work capacity.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:312 defines TC-016, but the repository only has the older broad cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts coverage and no workers-single-pane.spec.ts or workers-dispatch.spec.ts dedicated to TC-016.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers-single-pane.spec.ts covering node-wide worker listing, worker detail, freshness, and federation-scope listing fixture expectations.
- Add cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers-dispatch.spec.ts covering count=2 startWorker, workers.max_count refusal, stop worker, lifecycle/audit visibility, and live update behavior.
- Reuse or extend existing frontend E2E fixture helpers instead of relying on the developer live DDx state.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement production UI or server behavior in this bead except minimal fixture/test helper changes needed to express the tests. Do not cover Axon, 2k-scale artifact performance, or FEAT-029 managed-node outbound channels.

acceptance:
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves a server-managed drain worker has nonzero PID/PGID in WorkerRecord and status.json.
2. TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants uses fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children, then proves Stop leaves none of those descendants running.
3. TestManagedWorkerWatchdogReapKillsProcessTree proves watchdog reap follows the same SIGTERM-to-SIGKILL cleanup path and releases the claimed bead.
4. TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees proves server/manager shutdown cleans all server-owned worker process groups.
5. TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent calls Stop twice and proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup|TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants|TestManagedWorkerWatchdogReapKillsProcessTree|TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees|TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent' passes.
7. make install is run after the code change lands.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A server-managed worker must be fully cleanable. Stop, watchdog reap, restart, and server shutdown must not leave ddx work, claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants behind.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 says WorkerManager manages in-process work workers as goroutines. cli/internal/server/workers.go:438 creates a cancelable context, but no OS process boundary. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234 reads WorkerRecord.PID and :1282-1286 terminates a process group only when pid > 0. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1453-1457 has the same limitation in watchdog reap. Because server-owned workers are often goroutines with PID zero, the server cannot guarantee child harness cleanup.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce a server-managed process boundary. Preferred implementation: spawn a child ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group and record PID/PGID in WorkerRecord. If a transitional in-process path remains for tests, every harness child process group must be registered on the worker handle and killed through the same cleanup helper. Stop/reap/shutdown should send SIGTERM, wait WatchdogKillGrace, SIGKILL remaining descendants, release bead claims, persist terminal status, and be idempotent.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.
title: server: terminate managed worker process trees without leaks

description:
PROBLEM
The drain-queue golden tests are failing because the workers page now exposes more than one heading named `Workers`, and the Playwright spec is using a non-unique role query. `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts:306-410` calls `getByRole('heading', { name: 'Workers' })`, but the page also renders `Reported workers`, which makes the selector ambiguous in strict mode.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts:306-410` uses a heading locator that collides with the page’s section heading.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:1-120` and its rendered output define the page structure that exposes both the main page heading and the workers subsection heading.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the drain-queue test target the unique page heading or otherwise disambiguate the query so the golden flow can verify the worker dispatch and terminal-state assertions. Keep the fix confined to the drain e2e spec and any minimal accessibility label adjustment required to make the heading unique.

NON-SCOPE
- Worker dispatch semantics.
- GraphQL or backend worker state transitions.
- Other e2e specs.

ACCEPTANCE
1. Both drain-queue golden tests in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts` pass.
2. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

acceptance:
1. ExecuteLoopSpec struct defined in cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go. Exported fields include at minimum: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion. Audit: any field present in cobra extraction (agent_cmd.go:1582-1605) or workers.go:25-49 not in this list is a bug.

2. The unified spec does not contain a PollInterval field, a poll_interval JSON tag, or any public compatibility field for the removed polling flag. New CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.

3. parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) exists in the new package. Called only from cobra RunE in agent_cmd.go. No parallel cobra-extraction block remains. DispatchOptions holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) and is not persisted to spec.json.

4. parseExecuteLoopSpec maps --once to Mode=once, maps --watch to Mode=watch, defaults to Mode=drain, maps --idle-interval to IdleInterval only for Mode=watch, and returns a usage error when --idle-interval is provided without --watch.

5. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() methods exist. Lifecycle enforced: (a) client calls Validate() before HTTP submit or inline dispatch; (b) server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker calls ApplyDefaults() then Validate() after decode, before StartExecuteLoop; (c) spec.json on disk is fully resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran at write time).

6. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2390) decodes directly into ExecuteLoopSpec; the anonymous request struct (currently lines 2395-2409) is deleted. Mode, IdleInterval, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, and FromRev survive the REST round-trip.

7. graphql_adapters.go anonymous request struct (lines 63-76) and its parallel StartExecuteLoop literal (lines 127-141) are deleted. GraphQL dispatch goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as the REST handler.

8. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is replaced by or aliased to ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json files written by StartExecuteLoop use the unified struct and emit Mode plus IdleInterval.

9. runWorker (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD as the cost-cap ceiling, with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD fallback when zero. No hardcoded DefaultMaxCostUSD reference outside the fallback case.

10. OpaquePassthrough: server-submitted spec with OpaquePassthrough=true skips both validation gates - workers.go:357-361 (StartExecuteLoop pre-flight) and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch inside runWorker. TestStartExecuteLoop_OpaquePassthrough_SkipsValidation asserts that StartExecuteLoop with OpaquePassthrough=true does not call ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService and returns a WorkerRecord without error even when the harness name is unresolvable.

11. Reflection round-trip test: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection in cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or executeloop sub-package. Uses reflect to iterate exported fields of ExecuteLoopSpec; for each field sets a non-zero sentinel value; marshals to JSON; unmarshals; asserts equality. Test fails if any exported field has a zero-value sentinel after unmarshal. A future added field automatically falls under coverage.

12. Backward compatibility for disk only: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ load without error. Files containing unknown legacy fields are tolerated and ignored by json.Unmarshal default behavior. Do not enable json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads.

13. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field present. Server returns HTTP 400 with a capability description when incoming SpecVersion > server-supported version. SpecVersion==0 (unset) is treated as the current version. TestHandleStartExecuteLoopWorker_HighSpecVersion_Returns400 covers this.

14. Regression: inline execution path behavior is unchanged except for the explicit once/drain/watch mapping. Existing tests that cover the inline path pass without modification.

15. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... green.

16. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
ExecuteLoopSpec fields are declared in parallel structs across three independent code sites. When a flag or worker mode is added to cobra, the developer must also update the REST handler request struct in server.go and the GraphQL adapter request struct in graphql_adapters.go; missing one silently drops the field at runtime. The new work-loop contract requires one vocabulary across CLI parsing, worker persistence, REST, and GraphQL: mode plus idle_interval, with no public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag.

ROOT CAUSE
Each work field traverses six independent declaration points with no compile-time linkage:

1. cobra flag extraction plus local vars: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts fromRev, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once/watch mode, idleInterval, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, and maxPower as naked locals.

2. positional-param pass-down: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1744-1773 closes over these locals and assembles config.CLIOverrides by hand; no single struct groups them.

3. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec definition: cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is hand-maintained in parallel with cobra and has historically missed fields.

4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct: cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 is a separate anonymous struct, so newly added fields can be silently lost for server-spawned workers.

5. StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 plus cli/internal/server/workers.go:345-361 hand-assembles the worker spec; any field absent in step 4 cannot appear here.

6. GraphQL adapter request struct plus StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141 is a third parallel declaration; fixes to REST can leave GraphQL broken.

The consumer (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-926 runWorker) reads MaxCostUSD from escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD hardcoded (line ~753) instead of spec.MaxCostUSD because the field was never plumbed through. OpaquePassthrough has two validation skip gates that must both fire (workers.go:357-361 and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch in runWorker).

PROPOSED FIX
- cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go: define ExecuteLoopSpec with all fields currently spread across the six layers. Fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion int. JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.
- Mode is the canonical once/drain/watch selector. CLI --once maps to Mode=once. CLI --watch maps to Mode=watch. Default is Mode=drain.
- IdleInterval is meaningful only for Mode=watch. --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error in the cobra layer and must not be represented as a separate polling mode in the persisted spec.
- Do not add, accept, document, or emit a public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag. Historical worker records may be read best-effort as unknown/legacy input, but new CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.
- Add Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults(). Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) as the single cobra-to-spec conversion.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605: replace naked-local extraction with parseExecuteLoopSpec. Remove positional-param closure pattern; pass spec by value.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with (or alias to) ExecuteLoopSpec from the new package. Delete the parallel struct.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409: delete the anonymous request struct in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker; decode directly into ExecuteLoopSpec. Call ApplyDefaults() then Validate() before StartExecuteLoop.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141: delete the parallel anonymous struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal; route through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as REST.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:~753: replace hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD with spec.MaxCostUSD, with DefaultMaxCostUSD as fallback when zero.
- cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec_test.go: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection uses reflect over exported fields; sets non-zero value per field; marshals; unmarshals; asserts round-trip. New field = automatic coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7) owns replacing runWorker with exec ddx work; this bead does not change the subprocess vs in-process dispatch decision.
- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, MCP agent dispatch) may have the same smell; covered by sister bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string is owned by ddx-9d55601f; keep LabelFilter as string.

SEQUENCING
ddx-29058e2a (this bead) vs ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7 - server-spawn migration to exec ddx work): lands independently and before ddx-eccc6efb. ADR-022 says ddx-29058e2a remains valuable as worker-side struct unification (cobra to in-process flow) and should land independently. When ddx-eccc6efb lands later, it will delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec from workers.go as part of the exec migration; the unified ExecuteLoopSpec should already live in the durable agent package. If ddx-eccc6efb lands first, the scope of this bead narrows to the cobra and worker-facing layers that still exist.
