Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages. - Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE. ## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human: Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct. Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one. Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded. Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick. ## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage 1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task. 2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway). 3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate. 4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped. ## What this attempt did NOT do (and why) - No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice. - No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above. - No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

## Summary

The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector.

These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either:
  (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or
  (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned.

The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make.

## Evidence

1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims).
2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`.
3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites).
4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec.
5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands.

## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict)

The bead also fails the size check on its own:

- 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run).
- Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals.
- File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages.
- Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE.

## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt

The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human:

  Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct.
  Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one.
  Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded.

Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick.

## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage

1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task.
2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway).
3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate.
4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped.

## What this attempt did NOT do (and why)

- No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice.
- No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above.
- No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

acceptance:
1. New test (e.g. TestExecuteBead_TrackerCommitIndexContentionIsTransientNotBeadFailure): simulate the tracker commit at execute_bead.go:2286 hitting "fatal: unable to write new index file: exit status 128" (and index.lock) under a held lock; assert the bead is NOT marked failed and the tracker commit retries/succeeds on a subsequent attempt.
2. The tracker-commit path and the durable-audit-commit path share one transient-contention classifier (no divergent copy); a test asserts both call-sites treat "unable to write new index file" as transient.
3. Regression: a genuine non-transient tracker-commit failure (e.g. permission) still fails the bead as before.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBead_TrackerCommit|TestIsTransientGitContention' -count=1 green.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (captured live, 2nd 2-worker collision): With two `ddx work --watch` workers on /Users/erik/Projects/ddx, the DURABLE-AUDIT commit path correctly retried index contention (ddx-82c89a10: "transient git index/ref contention committing durable audit outputs ... will retry next iteration" -> merged), BUT a SEPARATE commit path — the tracker commit at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2286 (`return fmt.Errorf("committing tracker: %s: %w", ...)`) — does NOT route through the transient-retry, so the SAME "fatal: unable to write new index file: exit status 128" contention FAILED 3 beads outright: ddx-3fd19af1, ddx-122e0ec5, ddx-e6f047cd ("committing tracker: fatal: unable to write new index file: exit status 128"). So the resilience added for the durable-audit commit (ddx-23ac2796, and pending ddx-83361480/ddx-e8bf0695) does NOT protect the tracker-commit call-site.

ROOT CAUSE: the tracker commit at execute_bead.go:2286 (and its caller) returns the git error directly as a hard failure; it does not classify "unable to write new index file" / index.lock / "signal: killed" as transient nor retry, unlike the durable-audit path's finalizeDurableAuditOrStop + isTransientGitContention (execute_bead_loop.go:1026,1288). Two workers committing tracker state to the same shared .git/index contend, and the loser fails its bead instead of retrying.

PROPOSED FIX: Route the tracker commit through the SAME transient-contention handling as the durable-audit commit — classify index-write/index.lock/ref-lock/SIGKILL/deadline contention (via the shared isTransientGitContention, extended by ddx-83361480) as transient and retry on a later iteration (the tracker commit should be idempotent — re-deriving and re-committing the same staged state), rather than failing the bead. Prefer a single shared resilient-commit helper used by BOTH the durable-audit path and the execute_bead.go:2286 tracker path so the two cannot diverge again.

NON-SCOPE: the durable-audit classifier/deadline (ddx-83361480, ddx-e8bf0695 — this bead reuses that classifier); worktree/file leaks (ddx-b320e794, ddx-274943fc).
title: execute-bead: 'committing tracker' commit path lacks transient-retry on index contention — fails beads under 2-worker load

description:
PROBLEM
The new basic server-operator workflows need a repeatable reliability gate that can be run without scale/Axon noise or generated artifact dirtiness.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:383 defines TC-021 for the hermetic E2E gate.
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:111 defines Wave 5 as wiring the new E2E specs into the functional gate while keeping scale/perf and Axon separate.
- Previous frontend functional runs showed unrelated scale fixture failure and generated artifacts can dirty the tree, so this basic workbench gate needs a scoped command.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add or update frontend test scripts/config so the five workbench specs can be run as a basic functionality gate.
- Ensure fixtures use temp projects and isolated XDG_DATA_HOME.
- Ensure the gate does not leave generated videos/screenshots/build artifacts in tracked paths.
- Keep scale/performance and Axon tests outside this basic gate.

NON-SCOPE
Do not fix unrelated scale fixture failures in this bead. Do not implement production behavior beyond test/gate wiring.
title: test: wire server operator workbench e2e gate

acceptance:
1. .ddx/config.yaml documents bead_tracker.backend: axon plus the Axon connection block.
2. TestNewStore_DefaultsToJSONL, TestNewStore_SelectsAxonFromConfig, and TestNewStore_PreservesExternalBackends are added or updated.
3. config selection honors axon without requiring DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL, while jsonl remains the default.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.
- Subscription transport.

description: codex/claude blocking: failed and (later) local attempts must run in isolated git worktrees with a defined cleanup/revert contract; the worker must NOT drain a dirty/WIP repo (helix churned on this). PROBLEM: lifecycle dispatch rejects dirty roots (correct) but there is no clean isolation/cleanup contract for failed-then-escalated attempts, and dirty WIP repos churn the queue. PROPOSED: ensure every attempt (incl. escalation retries) runs in an isolated worktree; on failure, revert/clean the worktree before the next attempt; never operate on a dirty canonical checkout; define artifact retention. This must land BEFORE escalation + local mixin. cross-ref FEAT-004 addendum.

description:
PROBLEM
The Workers page should be the normal operator surface for queue-drain workers, including desired count, restart, and managed-vs-external state. Today it exposes start/stop/add controls but not durable supervision.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:12 defines START_WORKER_MUTATION, :22 defines STOP_WORKER_MUTATION, and :34 defines ADD_WORKER_MUTATION. Lines :57-72 derive only running drain count and max_count. There is no desired-count stepper, restart button, managed/external grouping, stale/backoff state, or warning that external workers are reported-only.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the Workers page to use the new GraphQL worker desired-state and restart operations. Show desired count vs actual managed count, a compact stepper for desired count, explicit Restart for managed workers, cleanup-needed/backoff/stale states, and a separate reported external workers section where stop/restart are unavailable. Keep the UI dense and operational rather than landing-page-like.

NON-SCOPE
Do not redesign unrelated navigation or document pages. Do not change server behavior beyond the GraphQL operations from the dependency bead. Do not add decorative marketing UI.

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a, slice 3 of 4)

Without `--global`, install and update must target the project's ddx root, which differs by mode: in-tree (`<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>`) versus convention (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>`). Either way the project-tier agent skill links (`<project>/.claude/skills` and `<project>/.agents/skills`) must point into whichever plugins tree is active.

ROOT CAUSE
- `resolveInstallDirs(global=false)` (cli/cmd/install.go:184, project branch install.go:195-197) sets `installRoot = ddxroot.Path(ctx, f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs to `<workingDir>/.agents/skills` and `<workingDir>/.claude/skills`. `ddxroot.Path` (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75) returns the in-tree `.ddx` path when `<project>/.ddx` exists and the convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` path otherwise, so mode selection is implicit.
- `installLocal` non-global branch derives `ddxRootDir := ddxroot.Path(...)` (install.go:453) for the same routing.
- Update: the non-global path `performUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:204) uses `ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, ...)` (e.g. update.go:227) so it updates the project install in whichever mode is active; mode is not an explicit flag but follows `ddxroot`.

PROPOSED FIX
Ensure `ddx install <name>` (no `--global`) in in-tree mode writes to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and links `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`; in convention mode (no `<project>/.ddx/`) writes to the XDG project plugins path and links the project-tier skill dirs into that path. Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.

NON-SCOPE
- The `--global` install/update surface (slice 2).
- `ddx init` behavior and doctor reporting (slice 4).
- Removing either mode — in-tree and convention coexist.

DEPS
- Slice 1 (plugin lookup precedence) and ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project paths route through `ddxroot.Path()`.
title: project install surface: ddx install (no flag) and ddx update honor in-tree vs convention mode

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills/personas/plugins can live in two install layers (project-scoped under ddxroot.Path() and machine-wide under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/). Runtime callers need a single authoritative resolver that picks the right on-disk plugin directory with a defined precedence, otherwise the dual-location install surface has no consumer and project installs cannot shadow global ones.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:22 ResolvePlugin must implement precedence project → global → baked-in. The project path is filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), "plugins", name) (resolve.go:23); the global path is filepath.Join(ddxroot.GlobalDir(), "plugins", name) (resolve.go:28); baked-in (defaultplugin.FS()) is valid only for name == "ddx" (resolve.go:33). ddxroot.GlobalDir() is defined at cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:90.
- Before this layer existed, install/update targeted ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and there was no precedence rule, so an operator with both a project and a global copy had undefined resolution.

PROPOSED FIX
1. ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name) returns (path, layer, err): when both project and global copies exist, return the project path with layer "project"; when only the global copy exists, return its path with layer "global"; when neither exists and name == "ddx", return ("", "baked-in", nil) so callers fall back to defaultplugin.FS(); otherwise return a non-nil error naming all three layers.
2. The project path resolves through ddxroot.Path() so it is correct in BOTH in-tree mode (<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>) and convention mode (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/<name>).
3. SPEC IMPACT: file/update the skill-install-topology ADR (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent) documenting the three-layer precedence, and update the CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section to state the project-over-global-over-baked-in lookup precedence.

NON-SCOPE
- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).
- Does NOT migrate any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Does NOT add agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- Writing the plugin content / creating agent-tier links is handled by the install and init child beads, not here.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project path routes through ddxroot.Path() and the global path through ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on. Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2: skills/personas install-to-agent vs install-in-project).
title: registry: plugin lookup precedence resolver (project → global → baked-in) + topology spec

description:
PROBLEM
The Niflheim incident on 2026-06-08 showed that DDx cleanup/recovery can still leave heavy DDx-owned execution processes behind even when file cleanup exists. Existing cleanup removes stale temp worktrees, run-state files, evidence/logs, worker dirs, and scratch dirs, but the operator recovery command does not report or reap stale attempt-descendant processes such as orphan shells, `cargo test`/benchmark processes, or long-lived test servers whose cwd or command context still points at a DDx execution worktree. Those processes can keep burning CPU or hold ports after the original `ddx try`/`ddx work` attempt is gone.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/cleanup.go:69-72` constructs `agent.NewExecutionCleanupManager` and runs it in dry-run or `--apply`, but `cleanupCommandReport` at `cli/cmd/cleanup.go:11-30` has no process findings, PIDs, process-group counts, or port/process recovery fields.
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` cleans DDx temp dirs, registered worktrees, scratch roots, stale run-state files, evidence dirs, agent logs, worker dirs, and candidate refs. `ExecutionCleanupSummary` at `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` reports file/resource counts only; it cannot tell an operator that a stale DDx-owned process tree is still alive.
- `cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go:218-267` removes stale `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*` sidecar dirs, but it does not terminate or report the underlying DDx-owned process tree when a stale sidecar corresponds to an already-orphaned attempt descendant.
- `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:42-152` is an important closed fix for `ddx work` startup, but it only considers PID-1 children whose command basename looks like a top-level harness (`claude`, `codex`, `gemini`, `opencode`, `pi`) and whose bead lease owner PID is gone (`orphan_harness_reaper.go:82-100`, `155-168`). It does not cover the case where the harness root has already exited but a shell, cargo test/benchmark, or test server remains under the stale execution worktree/process group.
- `cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29` sets Setpgid/Pdeathsig for normal harness children, and closed beads `ddx-0d7d5f0e`, `ddx-3113d524`, and `ddx-418c3849` cover parent-death/startup harness reaping. This bead is not a duplicate: it adds an operator-visible cleanup/process-census path for stale DDx-owned descendant processes that no longer look like a live top-level harness.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the cleanup manager with a conservative process census/reaper for DDx-owned execution attempts. On Linux, use `/proc`-style facts already used by `cli/internal/workerstatus`/`orphan_harness_reaper`: pid, ppid, process group when available, command, cwd, start time, and enough ownership context to tie the process to the current project execution root or a known DDx attempt worktree. Provide a no-op/report-unavailable implementation on unsupported platforms.
- `ddx cleanup` dry-run must report stale DDx-owned attempt processes without killing them. The report should include PID/process group, command basename or truncated command, cwd/worktree, bead/attempt when inferable, liveness reason, and whether `--apply` would terminate it.
- `ddx cleanup --apply` may terminate only process groups that are provably stale and DDx-owned: associated run-state/cleanup liveness is expired or missing, the recorded owner PID is gone, the worktree is under the current project's execution root, and no live worker/registered active worktree still owns it. Prefer graceful terminate then bounded kill when feasible; never kill arbitrary host processes by command name alone.
- Wire the same conservative process pass into work startup/pre-claim cleanup so queue control can recover from stale attempt descendant processes before claiming more work. Keep cleanup lock/jitter behavior intact so multiple workers do not race to kill the same process group.
- Update cleanup reporting and JSON shape with process counts and blocked warnings. If a stale DDx-owned process is detected but cannot be killed safely, surface a warning/operator-visible observation instead of silently deleting files and leaving CPU/ports occupied.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go` and adjacent new platform-specific process cleanup files/tests.
- `cli/cmd/cleanup.go` and `cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go` for text/JSON reporting.
- `cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go` and existing work-startup cleanup tests for queue-control wiring.
- `cli/internal/workerstatus/*` or `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper*` only for reused process-scanning helpers, not broad rewrites.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md` if the implementation changes the documented cleanup contract.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Do not edit Niflheim.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-e0de6369`; that bead owns `.ddx/harness-sessions/` scratch ignore/prune behavior.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-bbea954f`; that bead owns dirty timeout worktree edit preservation before cleanup deletion.
- Do not replace the existing parent-death/process-group fixes from `ddx-0d7d5f0e`, `ddx-3113d524`, or `ddx-418c3849`.
- Do not implement a general-purpose port scanner or host process manager. Port conflict recovery here comes only from safely reaping stale DDx-owned attempt process groups.
- Do not kill processes outside the current project's DDx execution root, processes tied to live run-state/worker liveness, active registered worktrees, or non-DDx user workloads.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker reliability and Niflheim local gates).
Deps: none.

description:
PROBLEM
The deadcode RTA artifact reports orphaned helpers and test-only utilities across profile_select, service_run, triage_dispatch, and repair_prompt as unreachable from the production graph. Each needs an explicit WIRE-or-DELETE disposition before ddx-83440482 can close.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
.ddx/executions/20260515T210515-1a20052a/production-reachability-final.json reports dead: cli/internal/agent/profile_select.go:72 — WarmProfileSnapshotForProject (orphan) and SelectCheapestProfile (test utility); cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:581 — TestProviderConnectivityViaService and service_run.go:668 ValidateEffortForRunViaService (orphans never called from production or tests); cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go:25 — IsGitUpdateRefCompareAndSwapFailure (test utility, exercised only by TestIsGitUpdateRefCompareAndSwapFailure in execute_bead_report_test.go); cli/internal/agent/repair_prompt.go:24 — BuildRepairPrompt (uncategorized residual). A prior attempt (no_changes) recommended a dedicated 'test-utility disposition' bead because naive deletion breaks the tests that call these symbols.

PROPOSED FIX
For each symbol decide WIRE or DELETE: if a symbol has a genuine production role (e.g. BuildRepairPrompt on the repair-cycle path), WIRE it so the production graph reaches it; if it is a pure orphan or test-only utility with no live consumer, DELETE it and remove or rewrite the dependent tests (TestSelectCheapestProfile_*, TestIsGitUpdateRefCompareAndSwapFailure) so coverage of any surviving sibling behavior is preserved. For any symbol needing investigation beyond ~15 minutes, annotate `// wiring:pending <follow-up-bead-id>` and file the narrower follow-up before closing.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not touch the ratelimit_retry cluster (sibling bead).
- Do not touch preclaim_intake_hook / recovery_decompose / recovery_reframe wiring (sibling bead).
- Do not delete SelectCheapestProfile's sibling profile-selection logic still reached in production (e.g. SelectImplementationProfile paths).
- Do not touch candidate-cycle, compare, or execute-bead residuals.
- Do not change the production-reachability protocol or checker implementation.
title: checks: dispose orphaned profile/service/triage/repair helpers from reachability graph

description:
PROBLEM
Concurrent `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution is close to viable but still has several lock and worktree hazards that can prevent reliable multi-worker drain: slow setup can run under the main-git lock, post-land commands can run while that lock is held, cap enforcement can target a checkout-local lock path instead of the shared linked-worktree lock path, and the fast test suite does not yet pin the full TP-021 local-only reliability contract.

ROOT CAUSE
CURRENT STATE: docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-021-multi-worker-try-reliability.md captures the governing test plan. Implementation review found concrete gaps in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1499-1534, cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1208-1214, cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap.go:216-221, cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:38-50, cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:14-19, and cli/internal/bead/store.go:1081-1100.

PROPOSED FIX
- Implement the child beads under this epic so local-only chaos, integration, and performance tests cover the multi-worker workflow without network or external agent bottlenecks.
- Keep each child focused on one subsystem or test slice.
- Use the `script` harness and temp fixture repositories for subprocess integration tests.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement a new `ddx work --parallel` scheduler.
- Do not add hosted git, provider, Docker pull, or external model dependencies to the required test path.
- Do not change multi-machine coordination beyond documenting any contradiction found in existing specs.
title: multi-worker: harden ddx try concurrency and local reliability gates

2. Path A run: each fixture's bead replayed via ddx try (or ddx work --once) on a branch from base-rev~1; outcomes captured.
3. Path B run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with bead's auto-prompt verbatim; outcomes captured.
4. Path C run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with operator hand-curated prompt; outcomes captured.
5. Per-fixture, per-path measurements table with the 7 named metrics.
6. Aggregate stats + failure-mode distribution.

description:
Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.

description:
PROBLEM
A live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle "Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.

This bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.

BACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE
The evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.

1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title "Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing".
- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.
- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.
- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.
- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:
  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`
  `powerClass=standard`
  `rationale: status: open`
  `orchestrator_action: decompose`
  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`
  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`
  `outcome_reason=decomposed`
- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.

2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title "Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8".
- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.
- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.
- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:
  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.
- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.

3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title "Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata".
- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:
  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`
- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.
- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.

4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.
- Recent Niflheim "success" attempts had empty review results:
  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.
  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.
  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision="success"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.
- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.
- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.
- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.
- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.
- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.
- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.
- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.
- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.
- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.
- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.
- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.
- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.

2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (internal/registry/resolve_test.go) asserts that when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists, ResolvePlugin returns the global path with layer=="global".

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 5 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
Plugin content can live in two tiers — per-project (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>) and machine-wide global (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>). Runtime resolution must deterministically prefer the project copy when present, fall back to the global copy, and finally to the baked-in default (ddx plugin only). Without a single authoritative resolver, install/update/init/doctor each reinvent path logic and drift.

ROOT CAUSE
- internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the precedence resolver: project path computed at resolve.go:30, global at resolve.go:35, baked-in fallback at resolve.go:44. It must be the only place precedence is decided and must be covered by named tests so install/init/doctor can rely on it.
- internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name> via ddxroot.GlobalDir(); the project path routes through ddxroot.Path() (in-tree vs convention mode handled there).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Ensure `ResolvePlugin` returns (path, layer, err) with layer ∈ {project, global, baked-in} and the exact precedence project → global → baked-in.
2. Add table-driven tests in internal/registry/resolve_test.go pinning both the prefers-project and falls-back-to-global behaviors against a temp HOME/XDG fixture.
3. Do not alter install/update/init/doctor call sites here beyond confirming they call `ResolvePlugin` (those are covered by sibling children).

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the per-project install tier (both tiers coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- install/update/init/doctor wiring (sibling children).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — ResolvePlugin routes through both ddxroot.Path() and ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on before this lands.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md (precedence decision record).
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" three-layer precedence note.
title: registry: runtime plugin lookup precedence (project → global → baked-in)

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 3 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
`ddx init --global` is the umbrella first-time machine setup: it installs the bundled default ddx plugin globally, links the skill into the agent-tier paths so every harness on the machine discovers it, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults. Conversely, `ddx init` (no flag) must create <project>/.ddx/ WITHOUT installing any plugin — plugins lazy-resolve, preferring the project copy over global.

ROOT CAUSE
- cmd/init.go:74 routes `--global` to `initGlobal()` (init.go:318), which materializes the default plugin into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:319-328), links the skill (init.go:331), and writes global config (init.go:351-353, writeGlobalConfig at init.go:362).
- The no-flag init path must not write into any plugins/ tree; lazy resolution is delegated to ResolvePlugin (internal/registry/resolve.go:29).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Confirm `ddx init --global` produces ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/, plus ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml.
2. Confirm `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ but writes nothing under any plugins/ directory.
3. Cover both with named tests against a temp HOME/XDG + temp project.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the per-project install tier (both tiers coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- install/update flag wiring and runtime precedence internals (sibling children).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — init routes through ddxroot.GlobalDir() and ddxroot.Path(); both must be agreed-on before this lands.
Soft-depends on the ResolvePlugin resolver child for lazy-resolution assertions.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md.
title: init: --global one-time machine setup creates agent-tier links; plain init does not install plugins

description:
PROBLEM
  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.
- P3: the worker panel should surface conflict-recovery transitions and any degraded path.
- P4: a bad bead X must not spill into bead Y; conflict recovery stays per-bead.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx does not have a written contract that separates broad interactive queue stewardship from worker execution. An agent opened in the repo can interpret queue questions as permission to start edits, while ddx work is meant to pick up execution-ready beads.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy without declaring a default interactive queue-steward mode. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work layers but not the interactive mode boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work", "what's on the queue", and "what's ready" through the ddx skill without specifying whether those prompts mean human intervention triage or worker execution.

PROPOSED FIX
Update AGENTS.md, FEAT-010, and FEAT-011 with an explicit mode taxonomy: queue_steward for broad interactive DDx questions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead worker prompts, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Define precedence so bead_execution supersedes only the queue-steward default, while tracker, merge-policy, and safety instructions remain load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing CLI behavior beyond static guidance tests.
- Changing tracker status enums or storage.
- Starting or managing workers automatically from interactive sessions.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: none.
title: agent-modes spec: document interactive stewardship modes

description:
PROBLEM
  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/coordinator.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/review.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/review_triage.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/disposition.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go         -> cli/internal/agent/work/drain.go
    cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/mirror.go

  Rename identifiers: ExecuteBeadWorker -> work.Worker, ExecuteBeadWorker.Run -> work.Worker.Drain, ExecuteBead -> try.Attempt (FEAT-006 + FEAT-010).

  Add cli/cmd/try.go with cobra command "ddx try <bead-id>" invoking try.Attempt directly (FEAT-010 surface).

  Update FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 spec docs to reflect new package paths. Keep ExecuteBead* shims as typedefs; deletion is C14.

NON-SCOPE
  - Behavior changes to the drain loop or attempt logic.
  - Deletion of ExecuteBead* typedefs (C14).
  - ddx-dc157075 file-path reference migration if that bead has not yet landed.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: file and identifier names should reflect the subsystem boundary and keep responsibilities separated.
- P5: ddx try as a first-class CLI surface makes the worker/attempt state easier for operators to see.
- P7: the refactor aligns names and prompt surface with the bead-as-prompt contract.

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/bead/store.go exposes Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead) error, Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)) error, and Close(ctx context.Context, id string) error with typed parameters and no variadic argument parsing for those methods.
2. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadLifecycle.Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead) error while preserving Apply(ctx context.Context, id string, op Operation) error.
3. All Go callsites for Store.Create, Store.Update, and Store.Close pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
4. Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreCreate_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreUpdate_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreClose_RejectsCanceledContext.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestStore(Create|Update|Close)_RejectsCanceledContext' passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational Store mutation surface to accept context explicitly, but Create, Update, and Close still accept untyped variadic arguments. This hides cancellation behavior and forces the original ddx-464feba2 bead to mix Store API changes with a repo-wide caller migration.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:86-89 declares BeadLifecycle with Create(args ...any), while Apply already uses ctx-first shape.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:517-596 exposes Store.Create(args ...any), scans for *Bead, and calls GenID through the variadic path.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:627-655 exposes Store.Update(args ...any), scans for id and mutate function.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1499-1535 exposes Store.Close(args ...any), scans for id and then calls lifecycle helpers without explicit caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the mutation-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead), Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)), and Close(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadLifecycle in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only Create, Update, and Close callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests for canceled-context behavior and typed argument validation.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Read APIs: ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to Create, Update, and Close.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and read signature children.
title: bead store: migrate mutation APIs to ctx-first signatures

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 3 dependency-graph methods (`DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree`) and 3 queue-ordering methods (`QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`). Per TD-027 §6 the dep methods belong on `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` sub-interfaces (signature-only change — ctx added). Queue ordering is operator-driven workflow; per TD-027 §14 it belongs in `ops/queue/` helpers with typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. Foundation declared the `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` interfaces but ctx isn't yet on `*Store`'s dep methods. `BeadQueue` isn't an interface in TD-027 — queue ops are workflow concerns that live as helpers.

# PROPOSED FIX

## Dependencies (signature-only)

1. Update `*Store.DepAdd(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepRemove(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepTree(ctx, rootID)` to take ctx.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadDependencyReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadDependencyWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers. Narrow field types opportunistically (e.g., `ddx bead dep tree` CLI handler can narrow to `BeadDependencyReader`).

## Queue (helper-migration; methods removed)

4. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `QueueSetTop{}` — move bead to top of queue (sparse rank ordering).
   - `QueueSetPosition{Position int}` — operator move-to-position.
   - `QueueClearOp{}` — remove queue rank.
5. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go`:
   - `Top(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueSetTop{}`.
   - `Move(ctx, l, id, position int) error` — wraps `QueueSetPosition`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueClearOp{}`.
6. Migrate callers (operator queue manipulation in `cli/cmd/bead.go` queue subcommands) from `*Store.QueueTop`/`QueueMove`/`QueueClear` → `ops/queue/` helpers.
7. Remove `*Store.QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dependency cycle detection — handled at higher layer.
- Queue rank renormalization algorithm — internal to ops/queue; sparse-rank logic remains as-is.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `*Store.DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree` take ctx. Compile-time assertions for `BeadDependencyReader` and `BeadDependencyWriter` compile.
2. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares `QueueSetTop`, `QueueSetPosition{Position}`, `QueueClearOp` types with `Apply(b *Bead) error` impls. Tests: `TestOperation_QueueSetTop_AssignsTopRank`, `TestOperation_QueueSetPosition_RespectsBucket`, `TestOperation_QueueClearOp_RemovesRank`.
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/` package exists with helpers + tests: `TestOpsQueue_Top_MovesBeadToFront`, `TestOpsQueue_Move_PreservesPriorityBucket`, `TestOpsQueue_Clear_RemovesOperatorOverride`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes `QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package.
6. Queue-ordering invariants (SD-004 §"Ready queue" / queue derivation) preserved — operator overrides apply within a priority bucket; explicit rank wins over missing rank within bucket. Test: `TestQueueOrdering_OperatorRankWithinBucket`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:workflow, area:dep, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

Deps: ddx-c6317784.
title: D: Dependencies (ctx) + Queue helpers + Operations + remove 3 *Store queue methods (TD-027 §6, §14)

description:
PROBLEM
Interactive queue stewardship needs a higher-level surface than ddx bead ready. Ready beads are worker-actionable and should usually be left to ddx work; operators need a command that exposes work ddx ready/work will not pick up plus a conservative signal when the ready queue is deep enough to justify starting another worker.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:7-16 lists ready, blocked, status, and list as queue inspection commands but has no human-intervention view. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 answers "What should I work on next?" with ddx bead ready. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:119-160 prints execution plan rows and skipped epics, but not a first-class intervention queue or worker-capacity recommendation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a read-only command surface, proposed as ddx work focus, that summarizes human-required intervention separately from worker-ready work. It should show needs_human/review-blocked beads, blocked beads that require planning or dependency decisions, malformed or underspecified beads if lifecycle signals are available, ready-depth summary, active-worker/in-progress signals when observable, and unknown capacity hazards when not observable. It may recommend starting another worker only from observable queue depth/capacity signals.

NON-SCOPE
- Automatically starting workers.
- Closing, updating, or splitting beads.
- Replacing ddx work plan or ddx bead ready.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: ddx-377ebf92.
title: work focus: expose human-required queue intervention

description:
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational bead Store surface to become typed and ctx-first, but setup and ID generation still accept untyped variadic arguments. This preserves the legacy no-context API shape and keeps part of the oversized ddx-464feba2 migration bundled with unrelated callers.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:74-89 declares the TD-027 foundation interfaces, but BeadInitializer still exposes Init(args ...any) instead of Init(ctx context.Context).
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:134-141 implements storeCallContext(args), which allows context to be optional and hidden in variadic arguments.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:148-179 exposes Store.Init(args ...any) and Store.GenID(args ...any).

PROPOSED FIX
Change only Store.Init and Store.GenID to typed ctx-first signatures: Init(ctx context.Context) error and GenID(ctx context.Context) (string, error). Update BeadInitializer in cli/internal/bead/backend.go for Init. Migrate only Init and GenID callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly so the full module still builds under lefthook. Add deterministic bead package tests for canceled-context behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Read APIs: ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.
- Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to Init and GenID.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the read and mutation signature children.
title: bead store: migrate Init and GenID to ctx-first signatures

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

There is no first-time machine setup command, and `ddx init` bundles plugin install with manifest creation, forcing a plugin write into every newly initialized project instead of lazy-resolving from the global tier.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation; there is no `--global` umbrella path that installs the default `ddx` plugin globally and writes global config.

PROPOSED FIX
1. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella first-time-setup command: installs the default `ddx` plugin globally to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (via GlobalPluginDir), creates agent-tier links (~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.
2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and its manifest but does NOT install plugins; plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project tier over global per the registry precedence bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Install command `--global` flag (sibling install bead).
- Update command and doctor reporting (sibling update bead).
- The runtime precedence implementation (registry bead).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir) — init --global writes through it.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.
title: init: add `ddx init --global` umbrella setup and make plain `ddx init` skip plugin install
