description:
PROBLEM
The public DDx execution vocabulary is supposed to be `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work`, but large parts of the codebase still use legacy `execute-bead` / `work` names in public help, comments, server worker kinds, GraphQL models, tests, docs, tracker metadata helpers, and internal symbols. Operators now see mixed terms and developers keep adding new code under the old names.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1485-1563 still defines the legacy `ddx agent work` cobra surface as if it were primary: help says "work is the primary queue-driven execution surface", examples use `ddx agent work`, and deprecation text still names work rather than pointing to `ddx work`.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1590-1940 keeps the main work implementation named `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`, `writeExecuteLoopResult`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, so new work-path changes naturally copy the old terminology.
- cli/cmd/try.go:213-333 implements the new `ddx try` command by constructing `ExecuteBeadExecutor`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadWorker`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, so the new public command still routes through legacy names.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:2233-2281 and cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts:343-397 expose worker kind/comment strings as `work` / `execute-bead`, which leaks legacy naming through API and UI fixtures.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:215-262 still describes attempt results and retry metadata as `execute-bead` / `work-*`, even while FEAT-010 and ADR-023 name the lifecycle `try` / `work`.
- Existing related beads are partially stale or narrower than this repo-wide cleanup: ddx-387a0178 owns C13 file/identifier renames and still says `ddx try` does not exist, while ddx-29058e2a / ddx-1a9cc01f still propose `ExecuteLoopSpec` naming for a work-side spec.

WHY THE NAMES DIVERGED
The codebase grew from the historical `ddx agent execute-bead` and `ddx agent work` commands. FEAT-010 introduced the clearer layered vocabulary (`run` = invocation atom, `try` = one bead attempt, `work` = queue drain), and `ddx try` / `ddx work` now exist as the intended operator surface. The implementation retained old symbols and compatibility aliases, so old names persisted as the easiest local pattern to copy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Perform a repo-wide terminology audit using `rg -n "work|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead"` and classify each hit as: public/user-facing rename, internal compatibility alias to retain, durable metadata key to retain with documentation, historical docs intentionally retained, or generated fixture/test data.
- Update public/user-facing CLI help, comments, API/GraphQL descriptions, server/frontend labels, tests, and docs to prefer `work` and `try`.
- Rename non-compatibility internal types/functions introduced by the FEAT-010 path from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary. Coordinate with ddx-387a0178 C13; if C13 already performs a rename, fold this cleanup into or after it rather than duplicating work.
- For compatibility aliases and durable data keys that must remain (`ddx agent work`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `work-*` Extra keys, historical event kinds), add concise comments documenting them as legacy/back-compat names and point readers at `ddx work` / `ddx try`.
- Re-scope or update related open beads whose titles/descriptions still establish new `ExecuteLoop*` names (notably ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f) so future work does not add fresh legacy terminology.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove the legacy `ddx agent work` or `ddx agent execute-bead` command aliases in this bead; they are compatibility surfaces unless a governing doc explicitly authorizes removal.
- Do not rename durable tracker keys or historical event kinds if doing so would break existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions`, worker records, or archive reads. Document retained keys instead.
- Do not change work/try behavior, queue ordering, claim semantics, routing policy, or Fizeau integration.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; update any related bead metadata only with `ddx bead update` / `ddx bead dep`.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-387a0178: structural C13 file/identifier rename; this bead depends on it or should be folded into it if it is re-authored.
- ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f: ExecuteLoopSpec naming conflicts with the intended WorkSpec/WorkRequest vocabulary; update or supersede as part of this cleanup.
- ddx-eccc6efb: server-spawn migration may delete some `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` paths; if it lands first, shrink this bead to remaining public/API/doc names.
notes: Superseded by ddx-f088a3fd / ddx-b194d20f and audited by ddx-bdc0065e. This plan retains ddx agent compatibility aliases and legacy cli/internal/agent package targets, so it must not dispatch.
title: agent: replace legacy execute terminology with work/try nomenclature

description:
PROBLEM
The foundation bead for TD-027 split the bead storage migration into caller-facing and package-local slices, but the current `ddx-464feba2` child still bundles the Store API migration together with a repo-wide caller sweep. The local `cli/internal/bead` package work is still a valid, self-contained slice: `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` already declares ctx-first interfaces, while `cli/internal/bead/store.go` still exposes the legacy zero-arg methods (`Init`, `ReadAll`, `Get`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, `GenID`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:74-100` declares the ctx-first foundation interfaces.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:140-1181` still exposes the legacy no-ctx Store methods.
- `ddx-464feba2` was blocked because the broader caller migration exceeds that child slice.

PROPOSED FIX
Update only the core bead package surface so the Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` accept `ctx context.Context` as first parameter for the foundational non-event methods, and keep the verification local to `cli/internal/bead/...`. Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead. Update or add the store/package tests needed to prove the local API still behaves correctly after the signature change.

NON-SCOPE
- Caller migration in `cli/cmd`, `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/server`, `cli/internal/escalation`, and metrics packages.
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`).
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. The foundational Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` take `ctx context.Context` first: `Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, and `GenID`.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
4. Any tests added or updated in `cli/internal/bead/` are named and deterministic, with no caller-package changes in this bead.

events.body:
status=proposed
reason=bead AC1+AC3 contradict the explicit non-scope; needs operator re-scoping Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885): 1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go): Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149 GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167 ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182 ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224 Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518 Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598 Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627 Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426 And in archive.go: GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172 Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today. 2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`, etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in external packages. 3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead"; "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.") and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead." 4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129). That build covers every package in the module, including all the external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching. 5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`): - 174 Go files reference these Store methods. - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37, cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/..., cli/internal/server/...). - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too: cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0]) cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...) `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/ GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027 (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package first) without breaking the build outside the package. No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work outside the worktree.

Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885):

1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go):
     Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149
     GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167
     ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182
     ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224
     Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518
     Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598
     Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627
     Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426
   And in archive.go:
     GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172
   Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts
   either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today.

2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first
   parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in
   cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying
   AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`,
   etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in
   external packages.

3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external
   callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead";
   "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent,
   cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.")
   and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead."

4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs
   `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129).
   That build covers every package in the module, including all the
   external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching.

5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today
   (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`):
     - 174 Go files reference these Store methods.
     - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg
       `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37,
       cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/...,
       cli/internal/server/...).
     - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too:
         cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0])
         cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...)
   `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one
   of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/
   GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is
   no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current
   call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a
   single receiver type).

6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that
   preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170
   files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server,
   internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the
   "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That
   is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split
   (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason.

Operator action requested:

  Re-scope this bead to one of:

  (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE
      block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the
      original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if
      pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd,
      cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server,
      cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics.

  (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test
      ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and
      explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit
      will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The
      follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is
      complete.

  (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or
      retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first
      Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the
      legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration",
      then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the
      caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027
      (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package
      first) without breaking the build outside the package.

No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged
strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work
outside the worktree.

description:
PROBLEM (docs child of ddx-937de9fc)
The reintroduced two-layer install topology (global vs project, lazy resolution, agent-tier links) must be recorded in the specs. Note this deliberately REVERSES the 'ddx install --global retired / plugins project-local only' statement currently in CLAUDE.md (epic ddx-602ae31a zero-footprint, Principle 2) — the docs must reflect the new precedence.

ROOT CAUSE
- No ADR documents skill/plugin install topology.
- CLAUDE.md (repo root) Persona System section and the project-local-only note do not describe global vs project precedence.
- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md 'How this skill works' and library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (and the mirror under cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/skills/ddx/) do not document install locations / agent-tier paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add or update docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-skill-install-topology.md (or the next free ADR number) describing: global tier ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins, project tier ddxroot.Path()/plugins (in-tree vs convention), agent-tier links, runtime precedence project>global>baked-in, and that --global is reinstated.
2. Update repo-root CLAUDE.md: replace the 'plugins project-local only / --global retired' wording with the two-layer precedence and document it in the Persona System section.
3. Update library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md 'How this skill works' and reference/agents.md to document install locations and agent-tier paths.

This child should land AFTER the behavior children so it documents the shipped shape.

DEPS: ddx-18e55cd2, ddx-aedb009d, ddx-7c3893ad, ddx-c772fdf9, ddx-ac6d4c22.

description:
PROBLEM
The two principles of epic ddx-602ae31a (ddx home resolution + skills/personas install topology) must behave identically across both modes (in-tree and convention). Today there is no single test suite that drives the same scenarios through both modes; coverage is per-mode-ad-hoc and divergence between modes is invisible until operators hit it in production.

ROOT CAUSE
The two parent beads (ddx-06cbaa90 and ddx-937de9fc) each carry per-feature tests. Neither owns the cross-mode equivalence guarantee.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go (or equivalent suite under a new package) that parameterizes every behaviour-equivalence scenario across both modes:

  for mode in [Convention, InTree]:
    setup_fixture(mode)
    for scenario in [
      "bead-lifecycle":           create/show/update/close
      "bead-execution":           ddx try a bead, observe attempt evidence and state mutation
      "plugin-skill-resolution":  ddx-side plugin lookup honors precedence; agent-tier symlinks resolve to plugin content
      "persona-binding":          role → persona resolves correctly per binding config
      "worker-drain":             ddx work --watch picks beads and lands them
      "meta-prompt-sync":         CLAUDE.md (or equivalent) gets the right content
      "auto-commit-routing":      bead create/update auto-commit lands in the right git repo (project's vs XDG's)
      "attachments":              per-attempt event files written to the right anchor
      "index-lock-recovery":      gitlock recovery fires correctly in both modes
    ]:
      run scenario in current mode
      assert behaviour identical to the other mode modulo storage path

2. Fixture setup must be deterministic and isolated:
   - Convention mode fixture: tempdir as project root, no .ddx/; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed at a per-test tempdir; git remote set to a deterministic URL.
   - In-tree mode fixture: tempdir as project root WITH .ddx/ pre-created; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed elsewhere (must not be consulted).
   - Two clones of the same project share an XDG dir — explicitly tested as a separate sub-scenario.

3. Document the test-mode invariant in cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go's package doc: "Any behavior that diverges between Convention and InTree modes (other than the on-disk anchor) is a regression."

4. SPEC UPDATES — propagate the resolver/mode model to every doc that references .ddx/ paths or project state. Each spec update added in this bead's commit cites principle-numbered tags so future readers see the link:
   - TD-031 bead state machine — "Storage anchors via ddxroot.Path(); see ddx-06cbaa90."
   - SD-019 multi-project-server-topology — Project identity from `host/owner/repo`; reference ddx-06cbaa90.
   - SD-020 multi-machine-coordinator-topology — same.
   - ADR-021 operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path — reference ddx-06cbaa90.
   - workers-sessions-operator-surface.md — worker status paths through ddxroot.Path().
   - plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture — FEAT-010 paths through resolver.
   - library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md and CLAUDE.md — install precedence per ddx-937de9fc.
   - library/README.md — install topology overview.

NON-SCOPE
- New behaviours (every scenario in the matrix is already covered by ddx-06cbaa90 or ddx-937de9fc per-feature tests; this is the cross-mode equivalence layer).
- Migration tooling for existing in-tree repos (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
ddx-937de9fc (skills/personas/plugins).

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-215b08a2 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. Document cache keyed by graph revision exists; cold request builds once and subsequent requests with same revision use cache.
2. Concurrent requests for the same graph revision do not double-build (single-flight or equivalent).
3. documents resolver supports a search parameter; filtered results come from cache.
4. Cache invalidated on file change (mtime-based).
5. TestDocumentsResolver_Cache_HitOnSameRevision verifies warm request does not rebuild.
6. TestDocumentsResolver_Cache_MissOnRevisionChange verifies cache miss triggers rebuild.
7. TestDocumentsResolver_Search_FiltersFromCache verifies search uses cached data.
8. Cold + warm latency benchmarked via B7-C1 fixture (results committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/perf-cache-results.md).
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The server documents resolver rebuilds the full artifact graph on every page request, even when the underlying graph has not changed. On a 2000-artifact corpus, this produces visible latency on every load and makes concurrent requests redundantly expensive.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go (or equivalent resolver file): the documents resolver calls the full graph-build pipeline on every GraphQL request without checking if the graph revision has changed since the last build.
- No normalized artifact cache (by graph revision) exists — every request starts cold.
- The search path in the documents resolver also rebuilds the full artifact list on every search call.
- ddx-5681cc57 (2k fixture) is a dep: cache design must be validated against the 2k scale fixture.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a cache keyed by graph revision (mtime-based, as recommended in the perf epic): store normalized artifact list (lowercased title/path/id/mediaType) keyed by revision string.
- Add search to documents resolver: filter the cached normalized list by search query.
- Add invalidation on file change: detect mtime changes and invalidate cache entry.
- Ensure concurrent requests don't double-build (single-flight pattern or sync.Once per revision).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching for resolvers other than documents.
- CI performance gate (that is a later bead).
title: perf: cache graph/artifact metadata + add search to documents resolver + invalidation

acceptance:
1. `ddx bead dep add <child> <parent>` and `ddx bead dep add <child> <ancestor>` hard-reject before writing tracker state; stderr names the offending ancestor and prints the parent chain. Test: TestDepAddRejectsParentAncestor.

2. `ddx bead create --parent Y --depends-on Y` and `ddx bead create --parent child-parent --depends-on root-ancestor` hard-reject with the same error shape and no created bead row. Test: TestBeadCreateRejectsParentAncestorDependency.

3. Existing valid dependency graphs, including `parent -> child` edges used to hold decomposed parents until children close, still pass and remain claimable according to existing queue rules. Test: TestDepAddAllowsParentDependingOnChild.

4. Verification gate passes: `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestDepAddRejectsParentAncestor|TestBeadCreateRejectsParentAncestorDependency|TestDepAddAllowsParentDependingOnChild' -count=1`.

5. Repository gate passes: `lefthook run pre-commit`.
description:
PROBLEM: `ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>` can create `child -> parent` or `child -> ancestor` edges, which deadlocks decomposed parents because the parent is waiting on children while children wait on the parent chain.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/bead/store.go:2848` implements `Store.DepAdd` with existence, self-edge, and dependency-cycle checks only; it never walks `Bead.Parent` ancestry before appending the dependency at `cli/internal/bead/store.go:2888`. `cli/cmd/bead.go:328` wires `ddx bead create` and accepts `--parent` at `cli/cmd/bead.go:355`, but creation-time dependency handling does not share a parent-ancestor invariant with `DepAdd`.

PROPOSED FIX: Add a central tracker validation helper that builds the parent chain for the dependent bead or pending create request and hard-rejects any dependency whose target is the bead's parent or any ancestor. Use the helper from `Store.DepAdd` and from `ddx bead create --depends-on ... --parent ...` handling. Error text must name the offending ancestor and include the parent chain in stable order. Preserve normal dependency cycle detection for non-parent graph cycles.

NON-SCOPE: Do not change decomposition semantics for valid `parent -> child` edges. Do not auto-repair existing tracker rows in this bead; repair belongs to the doctor bead. Do not change work-loop retry policy or root-mutation logging. Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS: `docs/triage/decomposition.md:63` defines decomposed parent state; `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:182` defines the decomposition-first transition sequence.

INHERITED DEPENDENCIES: none specified by the parent bead.

1. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks asserts ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (in-project) does not write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve from global.
3. A Test* in cli/cmd/doctor_test.go asserts doctor renders both the "Global Install (...)" and "Project Install (...)" lines with the correct ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global statuses (doctor_test.go:273,276).
4. A test asserts the ddx-validate pre-commit hook runs skill validation only when skill files are staged, independent of install location.
5. The SKILL.md "How this skill works" install-locations note is updated in this bead's commit.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

First-time machine setup needs an umbrella `ddx init --global` that installs the default ddx plugin globally and wires agent-tier links, while plain `ddx init` must create only the project workspace and lazy-resolve plugins (preferring project over global) rather than eagerly installing. `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see where the skill resolves from. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09, already landed) must keep scoping skill checks to staged skill files regardless of install location.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:75 dispatches to initGlobal (init.go:318) when --global is set; initGlobal installs the bundled default ddx plugin into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:320), creates symlinks ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx into that dir (init.go:336), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (init.go:351, writeGlobalConfig init.go:362).
- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create <project>/.ddx/ and NOT write plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via the registry resolver, preferring the project copy over global.
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:421-448 computes globalPath/projectPath and the projectStatus "lazy-resolves-to-global" (doctor.go:436-444) and prints the "Global Install (...) — <status>" and "Project Install (...) — <status>" lines (doctor.go:447-448).
- Before this, `ddx init` bundled plugin install with manifest creation, there was no global setup command, and doctor reported only a single install layer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. `ddx init --global` installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx links into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx, and writes the global config with convention defaults.
2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins; first use lazy-resolves from the project layer, falling back to global.
3. `ddx doctor` renders two lines: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing" and "Project Install (<projectRoot>/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global".
4. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook continues to run skill-related validation only when skill files are staged, regardless of whether the install is global or project-scoped.

NON-SCOPE
- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).
- Does NOT migrate legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Does NOT add agent-harness skill paths beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- Plugin content writing for non-init installs is owned by the install child beads.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global setup routes through ddxroot.GlobalDir(), project init through ddxroot.Path(). Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2). Related: ddx-777f4a09 (ddx-validate hook, landed).
title: init/doctor: --global umbrella setup, lazy project init, and two-layer doctor reporting

1. Add `TestServerWarnsAboutOrphanHomeServerState` or the nearest package-appropriate named test proving startup emits a warning when `~/.ddx/server/` exists and the active `ddxroot.Path` destination is not `~/.ddx/`.
2. Add `TestServerWarnsAboutOrphanHomeTSNetState` or the nearest package-appropriate named test proving startup emits a warning when `~/.ddx/tsnet/` exists and the active state destination is not `~/.ddx/`.
3. The warning text identifies both the orphaned home path and the active state root so an operator can remove or migrate the stale directory intentionally.

description:
PROBLEM: After `ddxroot.Path($HOME)` stops returning `~/.ddx/`, existing incorrectly-created home state such as `~/.ddx/server/` and `~/.ddx/tsnet/` becomes orphaned. Operators need an explicit startup signal so stale TLS or tsnet state does not keep confusing client/server trust debugging.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` previously allowed `~/.ddx/` to act as a project state root. High-risk server consumers include TLS cert/key state at `cli/internal/server/server.go:326` and tsnet state at `cli/internal/server/server.go:503`. Once the resolver falls through to XDG, those old home directories are no longer read by the server path helpers.

PROPOSED FIX: Add startup detection in the server path that checks for legacy `JoinHome("server")` and `JoinHome("tsnet")` directories when the active project state root resolves elsewhere. Emit a clear warning naming the orphan source and the active XDG/project state destination. Keep the behavior warning-only unless the implementation already has an established safe migration helper.

NON-SCOPE: Do not rename `~/.ddx/` to `~/.config/ddx/`. Do not address multi-project federation collisions. Do not change TLS-renew scheduling or rotation logic. Do not silently delete legacy home-state directories. Do not move unrelated user-global state such as `installed.yaml` or `plugins/`.

acceptance: See bead body — 5 verifiable AC items: new ddxroot regression test, ~20 test fixtures updated to write config.yaml, go test -short ./internal/... green, orphan-warning at startup, pre-commit clean.
description:
**The bug**

`cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` resolves a project's state root in
one of two locations based on whether `<projectRoot>/.ddx/` exists as a
directory:

```go
func Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {
    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)
    if info, err := os.Stat(inTree); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
        return inTree    // <projectRoot>/.ddx/
    }
    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))
    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)
    return root          // ~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<identity>
}
```

The `.ddx/` directory test is too weak. It accepts ANY directory named
`.ddx/` as evidence that `projectRoot` is a DDx project. The home
directory `$HOME` happens to have a `~/.ddx/` directory because that's
where user-global state (`installed.yaml`, `plugins/`) lives via the
parallel `JoinHome()` helper. So if any `ddx ...` invocation is made
with `projectRoot=$HOME` (e.g., via `cwd=$HOME` and no `--project`
override), `Path()` returns `~/.ddx/` as if the user's home were a
project root.

Result: the same logical state files appear at two different paths
depending on how the binary was invoked, and the duplicates drift.
The concrete instance that triggered this investigation was the TLS
cert: `ddx server` running with `WorkingDir=/home/erik/Projects/ddx`
(via systemd) wrote its cert at
`/home/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem`, while a separate
invocation (probably manual, from `$HOME`) wrote
`/home/erik/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem` — different fingerprint, same
filename, same purpose. The server served the project-root cert; some
other client trusted the home-dir cert; every connection got
`tls: bad certificate`.

**Audit — every consumer of the dual-path resolver**

`ddxroot.JoinProject` / `ddxroot.Path` is called from ~50 callsites
across the codebase. Every one of them inherits the bifurcation. The
highest-risk consumers (those that read or write state that must NOT
diverge between locations):

- `beads.jsonl` (`execute_bead.go:2165`, `execute_bead.go:2722`,
  `execute_bead_dangling_success.go:189`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:244`,
  `service_run.go:396`, `server.go:797`, `server.go:5043`, `exec/store.go:56`)
- `metrics/locks.jsonl` (`lockmetrics/lockmetrics.go:96`)
- `metrics/attempts.jsonl` (`attemptmetrics/attemptmetrics.go:99`)
- `.git-tracker.lock` (`lockmetrics/lockcap.go:221`)
- `server/tls/cert.pem` + `key.pem` (`server/server.go:326`)
- `tsnet/` (`server/server.go:503`)
- `workers/<id>/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1370`, `workerstatus/liveness.go:44`,
  `executions_mirror.go:211`)
- `executions/<attempt-id>/` (`prose_evidence.go:125`,
  `reviewer_stream_artifact.go:41,45`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:23,27`)
- `agent-logs/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1268`)
- `config.yaml` (`init.go:155`, `execution_cleanup.go:221`,
  `execution_worktree_root.go:23`)
- `exec/definitions/`, `exec/runs/` (`exec/path_helpers.go:14,18`)
- `graphs/` (`docgraph.go:1197`)
- `run-state.json` + `RunStateDir` (`agent/run_state.go:60,64`)
- `tracker_lock.go:116` (the bead tracker lock)

Anything in this list reachable from two different `projectRoot`
arguments (or with one of them being `$HOME`) ends up duplicated.

**Proposed fix (structural)**

Tighten the in-tree check. A bare `.ddx/` directory is necessary but
not sufficient evidence of a project root. Require an initialization
marker: `<projectRoot>/.ddx/config.yaml` (the file written by
`ddx init` at `cli/cmd/init.go:236`). Patch:

```go
func Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {
    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)
    if isInitializedInTree(inTree) {   // was: os.Stat / IsDir
        return inTree
    }
    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))
    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)
    return root
}

func isInitializedInTree(dir string) bool {
    info, err := os.Stat(dir)
    if err != nil || !info.IsDir() {
        return false
    }
    _, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, "config.yaml"))
    return err == nil
}
```

`ExistingPath` gets the same treatment.

This makes `Path($HOME)` correctly fall through to
`~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<identity>` (since `~/.ddx/` has no
`config.yaml`), eliminating the dual-location bifurcation entirely.

**Blast radius (this is what blocks landing the patch as-is)**

A surveyed initial spike against this patch made `internal/ddxroot`
tests pass, but exposed ~20 test fixtures elsewhere that build a bare
`.ddx/` directory and rely on the old "any `.ddx/` = project root"
contract. Failing packages from a quick `go test -short ./internal/...`
run:

- `internal/server/graphql` (9 failures: `TestAgentMetrics_*`,
  `TestNode_ResolvesBeadByID_ScopedToProject`, `TestNode_ResolvesExecRunByID_ScopedToProject`)

Files that need their test fixtures updated to also write a `config.yaml`
in the `.ddx/`:

- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_agent_metrics_test.go` (fixtureWorkingDir)
- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_workingdir_ctx_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go` (3 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_interrupted_attempt_test.go` (2 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_commit_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_worktree_cleanup_test.go`
- `internal/agent/serviceconfig_test.go`
- `internal/agent/drain_regression_test.go`
- `internal/agent/operator_prompt_backlinks_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go` (6 callsites)
- `internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go`
- `internal/agent/execution_resources_test.go` (3 callsites)

A central helper (e.g., `testutils.MakeInitializedDDxRoot(t, dir)`)
that creates `.ddx/` + writes a stub `config.yaml` would cap the diff.

**Migration**

Existing user-global state at `~/.ddx/server/tls/`, `~/.ddx/tsnet/` etc.
gets orphaned by the fix (they were created when `Path($HOME)`
returned `~/.ddx/`; afterwards Path uses the XDG path). The transition
is graceful for TLS (server regenerates a fresh self-signed cert) but
operators with manual config in those subdirs will need a one-shot
migration. Recommend:

1. Emit a startup warning when `~/.ddx/server/` exists alongside the
   XDG path, telling the operator to delete the orphan.
2. Or auto-move `~/.ddx/server/` → `~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<host-id>/server/`
   on first server start under the new resolver.

**Operational note**

The original sindri-dev TLS bad-cert storm (~36 hours, blocking the
entire ddx work queue) was unblocked by manually copying the home-dir
cert+key over the project-root location and restarting ddx-server.
That's a band-aid; this bead is the proper unwind.

**Severity: P1**

The bug is latent across most state files. The reason it manifested as
"server unreachable" rather than "bead tracker corrupted" is that
nobody was running ddx commands as `$HOME` against beads files
recently. Any operator who does (or any future feature that uses
`projectRoot=$HOME` for any reason) will silently bifurcate state.

**Acceptance**

1. `go test ./internal/ddxroot/...` includes a new regression test
   (`TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`) that verifies a `.ddx/`
   without `config.yaml` falls through to the XDG path.
2. All 20+ test fixtures listed above updated to write
   `.ddx/config.yaml` (or use the new testutils helper).
3. `go test -short ./internal/...` passes.
4. Startup or migration logic warns about orphan `~/.ddx/server/` /
   `~/.ddx/tsnet/` directories.
5. Pre-commit / lefthook green.

**Out of scope**

- Renaming `~/.ddx/` to `~/.config/ddx/` (separate cleanup).
- Multi-project federation collision (different concern, different
  layer).
- The TLS-renew schedule / rotation logic (orthogonal — the bug is
  not "the cert was rotated"; it's "two carts exist").

description:
PROBLEM
Operators need a single web pane showing every worker across local projects, plus ergonomic project-scoped controls to start and stop autonomous ddx work workers.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:157 specifies `/nodes/:nodeId/workers`, but `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/workers/+page.svelte` is absent.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:57 derives running drain count only from `data.workers` for one project.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:115 starts a single worker through `startWorker` and has no count-aware form.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:320 requires node-wide worker rows with project badge, current bead, phase, route/model, elapsed time, and freshness.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add the node-wide worker route and load function for `/nodes/:nodeId/workers`.
- Render all registered-project workers with node/project badges, state, current bead id/title, attempt id, phase, route/model, elapsed, freshness, and mode.
- Add detail navigation/drawer behavior with recent events, output, prompt, current bead link, attempt link, and stop action.
- Update project worker controls to request count, mode, harness/profile/effort, idle interval, and label filter through the GraphQL mutation once server support lands.
- Keep polling fallback or subscriptions for live row updates.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement federation forwarding in this bead beyond reading/displaying fields already exposed by the local or federated query layer. Do not implement Axon or managed-node outbound control.
title: web: implement node-wide worker workbench and project worker dispatch

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
Runtime plugin lookup must follow a single authoritative precedence — install-in-project (project tier) overrides install-to-agent (global tier), with the embedded default ddx plugin as the last-resort baked-in fallback. Without one resolver, install/update/doctor each reimplement path logic and drift, so a project-local plugin can be silently shadowed by a global copy (or vice-versa).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the intended single entry point. It checks the project path `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name>` (resolve.go:30), then the global path `ddxroot.GlobalDir()/plugins/<name>` (resolve.go:35), then returns layer `"baked-in"` with an empty path only for name=="ddx" (resolve.go:41), else errors (resolve.go:44).
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` defines the global-tier path `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>`.
- The precedence contract is currently under-tested, so regressions in ordering would go undetected.

PROPOSED FIX
- Guarantee `ResolvePlugin` returns layer `"project"` and the project path when the plugin dir exists in BOTH the project and global tiers (project wins).
- Guarantee it returns layer `"global"` and the global path when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists.
- Keep baked-in (empty path, caller uses `defaultplugin.FS()`) valid only for name=="ddx"; return an error for an unknown plugin present in no tier.
- Add table-driven coverage in cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go using `t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", ...)` and a temp projectRoot with an in-tree `.ddx/` so both tiers are exercised deterministically.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing how install/update/doctor WRITE plugins (covered by sibling beads).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution): `ResolvePlugin` routes through `ddxroot.Path()` and `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`; both must be agreed-on. No other deps — this bead is the foundation the install/update/doctor siblings build on.
title: registry: ResolvePlugin honors project→global→baked-in precedence with table tests

description:
# PROBLEM
The legacy `*Store.MigrateToAxon` method (now exposed via `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` in MIG bead, returning `ErrDeprecated`) writes JSONL files under `.ddx/axon/` rather than performing an actual JSONL→Postgres migration. Per the audit in `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`, production AxonStore deployment requires migrating existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl` corpora into Lakebase Postgres. The real importer is unimplemented.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/migrate.go` (function previously at `*Store.MigrateToAxon`, now folded into `*storeMigrator` per MIG bead) writes JSONL into a sidecar directory. The function does not connect to Postgres or invoke `AxonStore.Apply`/`AxonStore.Create`. Production deployment cannot use it to populate Lakebase.

# PROPOSED FIX

1. Implement a real importer in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent post-BL location). The importer:
   - Reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` if present) — uses existing JSONL parser.
   - For each bead, calls `axonStore.Create(ctx, &b)` to insert into Postgres. Idempotent on `ErrConflict` (skip + log).
   - For each bead, appends events via `axonStore.Apply(ctx, id, AppendEventOp{...})` per TD-027 §13 vocabulary (events come from `Extra["events"]` on the JSONL bead or from sidecar `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/events.jsonl`).
   - Tracks progress, supports `--dry-run` (counts without writes), `--limit N` (batched migration).
   - Verifies post-migration: read back beads from Postgres, assert field-for-field equality with source JSONL (sample-based for large corpora).

2. Expose via `Migrator` interface (added in MIG): rename/replace `MigrateToAxon` with the real implementation; same method name, different (correct) behavior.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` to support the importer with appropriate flags:
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --dry-run`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --apply [--limit N]`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --verify` (read-back + checksum)

4. Remove the deprecated JSONL-writing behavior. `ErrDeprecated` return from MIG bead is replaced by the real implementation.

# NON-SCOPE
- Schema-versioning migration ladder (separate concern; deferred until first v1→v2 breaking change).
- Real-wire integration test against actual Postgres — separate WIRE bead.
- Continuous sync between JSONL and Postgres — not in scope; this is one-shot migration.
- AxonStore itself — implemented in AX (`ddx-9c5bca8f`).

# Acceptance criteria

1. New file `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent) implements the JSONL→Postgres importer. Uses `axonStore.Create` + `axonStore.Apply` for inserts + event appends.
2. Existing `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` method returns the real importer's result (not `ErrDeprecated`).
3. CLI flags wired: `--to-axon --dry-run`, `--to-axon --apply`, `--to-axon --verify`. Test: `TestImporter_DryRun_CountsOnly`, `TestImporter_Apply_WritesToPostgres`, `TestImporter_Verify_DetectsDrift`.
4. Idempotency: re-running `--apply` on an already-imported corpus is a no-op (skips conflicts). Test: `TestImporter_Apply_Idempotent_OnReRun`.
5. Field-fidelity: every JSONL bead field is preserved in Postgres, including `extra` preserved-keys. Test: `TestImporter_PreservesExtras`.
6. Event ordering: events imported preserve monotonic `created_at` (TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8). Test: `TestImporter_PreservesEventOrdering`.
7. Attachment migration: sidecar files under `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/` are uploaded to Unity Catalog Volumes (or local-equivalent for testing). Test: `TestImporter_MigratesAttachments`.
8. Verification mode: post-import, sample N beads, read back from AxonStore, assert equality with source. Test: `TestImporter_Verify_RoundTripPassesOnCleanImport`.
9. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green (with httptest-served Axon-shaped GraphQL endpoint or in-memory backend).
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:axon, area:migration, kind:feature, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-9c5bca8f (AxonStore must exist), MIG bead (Migrator interface must exist).
title: IMP: JSONL→Postgres importer for production AxonStore migration (replaces legacy MigrateToAxon)

2. TestDDxRoot_DefaultsToProjectRootDotDDx asserts Path returns filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx") in the default (no XDG fallback yet) case.
3. Static check: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. This is the regression guard for the audit. (The check is wired into lefthook as a pre-commit rule.)
4. cd cli && go test ./... -count=1 green. All existing tests pass without modification.
5. The audit covers at minimum the 12 path-writers enumerated in the description. The bead's no_changes_rationale.txt (if used) MUST list every file modified by the audit, grouped by package.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes, including the new "no-hardcoded-.ddx-paths" rule.
description:
PROBLEM
Every place in the codebase that writes to .ddx/<something> today hardcodes filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...). This makes it impossible to relocate the DDx state to an XDG location for convention-mode projects without auditing and rewriting every call site. A leaked path means the convention-mode invariant ("no files written to project tree without a .ddx/ folder") breaks the moment that code path runs.

The full enumeration of touched paths is at minimum:
- .ddx/config.yaml
- .ddx/beads.jsonl
- .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl
- .ddx/attachments/<id>/
- .ddx/executions/<run-id>/
- .ddx/run-state/<id>.json
- .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl
- .ddx/.git-tracker.lock
- .ddx/plugins/<name>/
- .ddx/workers/<id>/
- .ddx/agent-logs/
- .ddx/scratch/
- and likely 10+ more across cli/internal/server/, cli/cmd/, library/checks/, scripts/

ROOT CAUSE
There is no single accessor that returns "the DDx root for this project". cli/internal/config/ has GetMetaPrompt() and similar config getters, but no DDxRoot(ctx) function. Every path-writer reaches into the file system directly.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a new package cli/internal/ddxroot with a single public function: `Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string`. Today returns filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"); a follow-up bead (E2) extends it to fall back to XDG when projectRoot/.ddx does not exist.
2. Audit every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment. Replace `filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...)` with `filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), ...)`. The audit must cover cli/cmd/, cli/internal/, library/checks/ (where applicable to ddx-side paths).
3. Add a build-time linter check (or a pre-commit hook lint rule) that fails the build if a new `filepath.Join.*"\\.ddx"` literal appears outside cli/internal/ddxroot/. Use go vet via a custom analyzer OR a simple ripgrep-based pre-commit hook (lefthook).
4. The resolver MUST NOT change behaviour in this bead: for any projectRoot today, ddxroot.Path returns exactly filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"). This bead is a pure refactor — no functional change.
5. Existing tests must continue to pass without modification. If any test fails, it has hardcoded .ddx in a way that bypassed the resolver — fix that test.

NON-SCOPE
- XDG fallback path (E2 bead).
- Convention mode bootstrap (E2 bead).
- `ddx init --global` (E3 bead).
- Manifest overlay (E5 bead).
- Migration of existing data (none required; behaviour is identical).

DEPS
None. This is the foundational refactor that the rest of the epic builds on.
notes: Superseded by epic restructure into 3 consolidated beads aligned to the two principles: (1) bulletproof ddx home resolution, (2) centralized vs in-project skills/personas. New beads pending.
title: config: DDxRoot resolver + audit every .ddx/ path writer

No performance baseline exists for the documents/artifacts UI at realistic corpus size (2000 artifacts). Without a baseline, the caching work (ddx-4a7eed8c) cannot demonstrate improvement, and CI has no reference point for regression detection.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/ does not contain a scale fixture for 2000 artifacts.
- No measurement of first-paint cold/warm latency, scroll smoothness, or search latency at this scale has been recorded.
- ddx-b9993722 (FEAT-008 + TP-002 measurement contract, a dep) established the measurement methodology; this bead applies it to the 2k scale.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create a synthetic 2000-artifact fixture under cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/scale/.
- Measure: first paint cold/warm, scroll smoothness (frame rate under scroll), search latency (50ms goal from parent perf epic).
- Record baseline measurements in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/perf-baseline.md.
- Do NOT add a CI gate (baseline only — gate added in a later bead after caching lands).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching implementation (ddx-4a7eed8c).
- CI gate (deferred).

acceptance:
1. All 15 children closed.
2. ExecuteBeadWorker.Run replaced by work.Worker.Drain (~30 lines).
3. 18 store-error-as-loop-exit smells eliminated; transient store error during outcome-commit logs and continues.
4. attempted/hookFailed maps deleted; pick derives exclusion from drain.result.
5. Routing preflight runs once at Drain startup, never per iteration.
6. ddx work mode semantics preserved: default is drain-until-no-ready-work; --once runs one ready bead then exits; --watch stays alive and sleeps for the idle interval when no execution-ready beads exist; --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error; no public compatibility alias remains for the removed polling flag.
7. ddx try <bead-id> CLI command exists.
8. File/identifier rename per package split complete; ExecuteBead* shims deleted.
9. JSONL event shapes (bead.result, execute-bead, review, routing, triage-decision, loop-error) unchanged - verified by C0 fixture baseline.
10. Server worker integration (cli/internal/server/) compiles and behaves identically except for the explicit once/drain/watch mode contract; tests green at every phase.
11. ddx-c6e3db02, ddx-b24e9630, ddx-cfedee8e, ddx-a8718bec, ddx-dc157075, ddx-f9acb86d, ddx-7dcbd40b, ddx-8b7ed960, and ddx-dc300be9 behaviors preserved per C5/C9/C10 absorption ACs.
12. FEAT-006 + FEAT-010 cross-refs updated to reflect new package paths and the mode + idle_interval worker-loop vocabulary.
description:
ExecuteBeadWorker.Run() is a 700-line function with 24 returns: 4 legitimate terminal exits, 1 legacy idle-loop exit, 1 routing-preflight-kills-worker exit, and 18 store-error escalations from the same handleOutcomeStoreError pattern repeated across outcome paths. The implicit state machine in the loop is real and coherent (Pick -> Gate -> Claim -> Execute -> Classify -> Reviewing -> Terminal) but written as conditional soup instead of explicit state.

## Three-package split aligned to FEAT-010

  cli/internal/agent/run/   - Layer 1: invocation atom (existing dispatch + runner)
  cli/internal/agent/try/   - Layer 2: bead attempt (worktree, prompt, single Execute, Land, conflict-recovery, post-merge review, triage)
  cli/internal/agent/work/  - Layer 3: queue drain (pick, claim, heartbeat, drain-loop control, stop conditions)

try.Attempt(beadID) returns sealed Outcome { Disposition (enum), Cooldown, ParkReason, RecordEvents }. Loop becomes approximately 30 lines: pick -> guard -> claim -> attemptWithHeartbeat -> applyOutcome. Single commitOutcome helper deletes the 18 store-error returns.

## Ordering: refactor-LAST per user decision

In-flight Group A beads (c6e3db02 rate-limit, b24e9630 no_changes, cfedee8e ladder wire, a8718bec cost-cap reviewer) land first against current code. dc157075 tactical worker-stay-alive lands first as operational bridge. The stdout/watch-mode beads (ddx-7d9e1e82, ddx-f9acb86d, ddx-7dcbd40b, ddx-8b7ed960, ddx-dc300be9) define the public work-loop contract this refactor must preserve. THIS refactor lands last and absorbs all intervening behavior into the new architecture. Per-child ACs explicitly call out which in-flight bead's behavior each child must preserve.

## Naming and public semantics

Package = try (not try_bead - Go idiom). Function = try.Attempt(beadID). Loop = work.Worker.Drain. CLI surface is ddx run / ddx try / ddx work. ddx work has three modes: once (one ready bead then exit), drain (default: run ready work until no execution-ready beads remain, then exit), and watch (stay alive indefinitely, sleep for idle interval when no execution-ready beads exist, and poll for newly arriving work). The removed polling flag is not preserved as a public compatibility alias; the durable vocabulary is mode plus idle_interval.

## Children

  C0  Pre-refactor JSONL fixture baseline for event-shape regression detection
  C1  try.Outcome sealed sum type + Disposition enum + ParkReason vocabulary enumeration
  C2  try.Attempt function shell (wraps existing executor; no behavior change)
  C3  Move review verdict handling into try.Attempt (180 lines from loop body)
  C4  Move conflict recovery into try.Attempt
  C5  Move no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, push-conflict (absorbs ddx-b24e9630, ddx-c6e3db02 behavior)
  C6  Single commitOutcome helper; deletes 18 store-error exit paths + handleOutcomeStoreError
  C7  Uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps; pick() derives from drain.result
  C8  Routing preflight moves to Drain startup (runs once, not per iteration)
  C9  First-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation + downstream-consumer audit for execution_failed:max-cost grep before relocation. Includes queue snapshot plumbing and human-review blocker pressure from ddx-7dcbd40b.
  C10 ddx work once/drain/watch modes + transient idle substate. Watch mode owns idle interval sleeps, per-idle queue status, and next-ready transition messaging from ddx-f9acb86d and ddx-8b7ed960.
  C11 Heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper
  C12 Single phase-event emission (replaces 4 sites)
  C13 File and identifier rename per package split + ADD ddx try CLI command + handle dc157075 file-path migration if it landed first
  C14 Delete ExecuteBead* shims; verify all callers ported off (grep audit)

acceptance:
1. All 5 child beads (E1-E5) closed.
2. Convention mode end-to-end test: clone a fresh ddx-using project (no .ddx/), run `ddx bead list`, verify (a) no files written to project tree, (b) XDG project dir created with git repo, (c) bead list returns convention defaults.
3. In-tree mode end-to-end test: run `ddx init` in a fresh git repo, verify .ddx/ created in worktree, verify subsequent commands route there.
4. Override precedence test: place .ddx/config.yaml with a non-default value, verify it overrides global + convention.
5. Path-audit lint: `rg "filepath\.Join\([^)]*\\.ddx" cli/` returns zero hits outside the DDxRoot package (regression guard).
description:
EPIC

Eliminate the .ddx/ footprint from arbitrary git repos using DDx, while keeping today's in-tree behavior available for projects that want it (DDx itself, HELIX, etc.). Make init effectively touchless: zero ceremony for the common case.

DESIGN PRINCIPLE

Single rule: presence of <project>/.ddx/ determines the DDx root.
- If <project>/.ddx/ exists → in-tree mode. Root = <project>/.ddx/. Operator commits or gitignores per their own .gitignore. DDx never manipulates .gitignore.
- If <project>/.ddx/ does NOT exist → convention mode. Root = ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/). The XDG directory is itself a git repo containing all DDx state for that project; the project's own git repo stays pristine.

PROJECT ID is the human-readable remote URL host/owner/repo triple, parsed from `git remote get-url origin`. SSH URLs (`git@github.com:easel/ddx.git`) and HTTPS URLs both resolve to the same path. Projects without a remote fall back to `local/<basename>-<abs-path-short-hash>/`. The slashes are filesystem-real, so the XDG path is browsable: `~/.local/share/ddx/projects/github.com/easel/ddx/`.

OVERRIDE PRECEDENCE at command time:
1. In-tree (<project>/.ddx/config.yaml) — top priority
2. Global (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml) — installed by `ddx init --global`
3. Convention defaults — baked into the binary

Each layer is a delta on the one below. A project with no overrides commits zero new files and writes zero files into its worktree.

ENGINEERING IMPERATIVE

EVERY path in the codebase that today reads or writes under .ddx/ must route through a single DDxRoot(ctx) resolver. No filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) calls outside that resolver. A regression linter asserts this invariant. The audit is the hard part of this epic.

KNOWN-HARD QUESTION (worth solving but not blocking):

When multiple worktrees of the same project (same remote URL) exist on one machine — e.g. `~/Projects/ddx` and `~/work/ddx-experiment` — they share the same XDG state. SHAs in events are unambiguous, but operator UX for "where do I cd to inspect this" is. Solution direction: each XDG project keeps a worktrees.json registry. One entry is marked master (operator pin or first-seen). Cross-references in audit logs and bead show output cite the master path.

CHILD BEADS

- E1: config: DDxRoot resolver + audit every .ddx/ path writer
- E2: config: convention mode bootstrap (XDG git repo + worktree registry)
- E3: config: ddx init --global installs user-tier skills/personas
- E4: config: ddx init creates .ddx/ in project, switches to in-tree mode
- E5: config: optional .ddx/config.yaml overlay for per-project overrides

NON-SCOPE
- Migration of existing in-tree repos (today's DDx repo, HELIX) is a separate concern. They stay in-tree mode by virtue of having .ddx/ already.
- Server-backed state (federation) is orthogonal. XDG state can be a git repo with a remote; pushing that remote IS the team-share mechanism.
- Manifest-only mode is rejected — the convention/in-tree split makes a separate manifest file unnecessary.
title: config: XDG-rooted DDx state — convention mode + in-tree mode + touchless init

acceptance:
1. preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict (or a verdict newtype) decodes readiness_checks entries whose verdict is a JSON bool true (coerced to canonical "pass"), JSON bool false (coerced to "fail"), a JSON string (trimmed passthrough), or absent/null (""), without returning a json type error.
2. The singleton-object case (readiness_checks as a single object, preclaim_intake_hook.go:333-338) and the array case (line 331) both accept a bool verdict.
3. A focused unit test in cli/internal/agent (e.g. TestPreClaimReadinessCheck_VerdictAcceptsBoolStringAbsent) decodes payloads with verdict=true, verdict=false, verdict="fail", verdict="ready", and an entry with no verdict field, asserting the canonical Verdict value and that decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult returns no error for each.
4. The existing fail mapping is preserved: a regression assertion shows verdict=false produces a failed readiness reason via failedReadinessReasons, identical to verdict="fail".
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes
description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal a readiness_checks response when an entry's verdict is a JSON bool. The worker logs the warn-only rejection 'decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string' and skips the bead. When the faulty payload is produced for every candidate, the whole queue idles (observed Fizeau 2026-05-18, identical fingerprint 15155a1c... across distinct bead_ids).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:75-80 defines preClaimReadinessCheck with 'Verdict string `json:"verdict,omitempty"`' (line 77). preClaimReadinessChecksPayload.UnmarshalJSON (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:322-352) decodes each entry into preClaimReadinessCheck via json.Unmarshal at lines 331 (array case) and 334 (singleton-object case). When the producer emits verdict as a JSON bool (true/false) instead of a string, json.Unmarshal returns the type error, the decode aborts, and the payload is rejected as system_unready.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the verdict field tolerant: introduce a custom JSON type (e.g. a 'readinessVerdict' string newtype with UnmarshalJSON, or a custom UnmarshalJSON on preClaimReadinessCheck) that accepts a JSON bool, a JSON string, or an absent/null value and coerces to a documented canonical string form:
  - JSON bool true  -> "pass"
  - JSON bool false -> "fail"
  - JSON string     -> trimmed value, passed through unchanged (e.g. "pass", "fail", "ready", "not_ready")
  - absent / null   -> "" (empty, same as today)
The downstream fail-detection failedReadinessReasons (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:782-794) already lower/trim-compares Verdict to "fail", so false->"fail" keeps fail semantics intact; true->"pass" is a non-fail verdict. Document the bool->string mapping in a doc comment on the verdict type.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add the shared JSON schema / CI drift gate (separate child), the same-fingerprint escalation (separate child), the claim-success-rate signal (separate child), or README docs (separate child). Only the decoder tolerance + its unit test.
notes: Salvaged from wedged ddx try (ddx-8f2e0ebf repro). Fix=readinessVerdict newtype (bool true->pass/false->fail, string trimmed passthrough, absent->""); array+singleton cases. Agent used --no-verify; verified independently: TestPreClaimReadinessCheck_VerdictAcceptsBoolStringAbsent green incl. false->fail regression (AC4); full go test ./internal/agent/... green (AC5); go build ./... ok. Landed at merge 854ef1817 (fix commit 4d3f829c0).
title: pre-claim intake decoder: accept readiness_checks[].verdict as JSON bool, string, or absent

description:
PROBLEM
First-time per-machine setup has no entry point, and `ddx init` eagerly installs the default ddx plugin into every project. The intended model is: `ddx init --global` does one-time machine setup (global default plugin + agent-tier links + global config), while plain `ddx init` only creates `<project>/.ddx/` and lazily resolves plugins from the global layer on first use.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` always installs the default ddx plugin (init.go:271-284 via `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)`), producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx` unconditionally.
- There is no `--global` flag and no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` writer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx init` (cli/cmd/init.go).
2. init --global: install the default `ddx` plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx` (via the install-global path from the sibling install bead), create agent-tier links `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into the global plugin dir, and write `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` with convention defaults.
3. init (no flag): create `<project>/.ddx/` and the manifest, but do NOT install any plugin — plugins lazy-resolve via `registry.ResolvePlugin` (sibling resolver bead), preferring project over global, on first use. Remove the eager install at init.go:271-284 from the no-flag path.

NON-SCOPE
- The resolver precedence itself (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution).
- install/update `--global` wiring (sibling bead).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Migrating any `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution); sibling registry-resolver bead; sibling install/update --global bead (reuse its agent-tier link helper).
title: init: add `ddx init --global` umbrella setup and make plain `ddx init` lazy-resolve plugins

acceptance:
1. TestInstallGlobal_WritesGlobalTierAndAgentLinks asserts `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs the plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
2. TestInstallProject_InTreeMode asserts `ddx install <name>` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name> pointing into <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>.
3. TestInstallProject_ConventionMode asserts `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

`ddx install` targets the project path unconditionally and has no `--global` surface. Operators who want one Claude Code install per machine must repeat the install per project, and project installs do not correctly distinguish in-tree vs convention mode.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go targets ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and writes agent-tier links only for the project. There is no `--global` flag and no branch for convention-mode project paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` to `ddx install`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx plugin show`. When `--global` is set: resolve target via the GlobalPluginDir resolver from the dependency bead (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/), write the plugin content there, and create symlinks (or copies where the OS requires) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read — ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name> (and ~/.codex/skills/<name> where the agent registry defines it). Skill discovery from a fresh shell must find the skill via the agent-tier path.
2. Without `--global`: write to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and create project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In in-tree mode the links point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>; in convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) they point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>.

NON-SCOPE
- The runtime lookup precedence itself (owned by the registry dependency bead).
- `ddx init` / `ddx update` changes (sibling beads).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir + precedence) — install consumes GlobalPluginDir.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.
- ddx-777f4a09 ddx-validate pre-commit hook: skill validation must still scope to staged skill files regardless of install location — do not regress that scoping.
title: install: add --global flag and honor convention vs in-tree project install paths

2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins (cli/cmd/init_test.go) asserts plain `ddx init` creates `<project>/.ddx/` and writes nothing under plugins/.
3. TestDoctor_ReportsInstallTopology (cli/cmd/doctor_plugins_test.go) asserts `ddx doctor` output contains a "Global Install (...) — ok|missing" line and a "Project Install (...) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global" line, and that the project line reads `lazy-resolves-to-global` when only the global install is present.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

First-time machine setup needs a single umbrella command (`ddx init --global`) that installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates the agent-tier links, and writes the global config. A plain `ddx init` must create the project workspace WITHOUT installing plugins (they lazy-resolve from the global/baked-in layers on first use). `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see whether a project install exists or is lazy-resolving to global.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx init --global` dispatches to `initGlobal()` (cli/cmd/init.go:75,318), which: creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:319-323), installs the bundled default plugin there (init.go:328), creates agent-tier links so every harness discovers the skill (init.go:314-316), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults via `writeGlobalConfig` (init.go:351-366).
- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create `<project>/.ddx/` and must NOT write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via ResolvePlugin (project→global→baked-in).
- `ddx doctor` topology reporting lives at cli/cmd/doctor.go:415 `reportSkillInstallTopology`, which prints `Global Install (<globalPath>) — ok|missing` (doctor.go:447) and `Project Install (<projectPath>) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global` (doctor.go:448), using `registry.ResolvePlugin` to detect lazy resolution (doctor.go:431-444).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Verify `ddx init --global` creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes the global config.
2. Verify plain `ddx init` creates the project workspace and writes nothing under plugins/.
3. Verify `ddx doctor` renders both the "Global Install" and "Project Install" lines, including the `lazy-resolves-to-global` project status when no project install exists but the global one does.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence implementation (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).
- The `--global` install/update surface and project install modes (sibling children).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — initGlobal and doctor topology both route through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.
title: init/doctor: `ddx init --global` umbrella setup, `ddx init` lazy (no plugin install), doctor reports both layers
