2. Existing behavior from `ddx-2965925e` and `ddx-b2846610` remains covered by reuse/alignment with their helpers or by waiting on those dependencies; this bead does not add a duplicate low-level index-contention or timeout implementation.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-bced4e39 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this is typed classification and reconciliation behavior, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineer
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, land/reconcile status, and any review status available for the land coordination scenarios.
- Existing behavior from `ddx-2965925e` and `ddx-b2846610` remains covered by reuse/alignment with their helpers or by waiting on those dependencies; this bead does not add a duplicate low-level index-contention or timeout implementation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: Classify land coordination failures without implementation reruns: part 2

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go between the existing NoReadyWork branch and the sleepOrWake call hosts the diagnose→remediate→rescan logic described above.
2. cli/cmd/work.go exposes --no-auto-supersede-close, --no-auto-epic-decompose, --no-auto-closure-reclassify flags; cli/internal/config defaults work.autoRemediations.{autoSupersedeClose,autoEpicDecompose,autoClosureReclassify} to true.
3. TestWorkIdle_AutoRemediatesAndRescansWithoutSleep: fixture with one mislabeled closure-candidate epic + one supersession leftover; ddx work --once auto-resolves both in a single loop iteration, emits loop.auto_remediated event with counts, no sleep, returns drained.
4. TestWorkIdle_AutoDecomposeUndecomposedEpic: fixture with one genuinely-undecomposed epic that satisfies all RC-6 preconditions; loop dispatches exactly one AutoDecomposeEpic call, child beads are filed, parent epic execution_eligible=false, loop re-scans and picks one child.
5. TestWorkIdle_AutoDecomposeRespectsCostBudget: --max-recovery-cost=0; loop diagnoses genuinely_needs_decomposition but does NOT dispatch; bead is surfaced as gated_by_budget_or_cooldown.
6. TestWorkIdle_AtMostOneDecomposePerIteration: fixture with three genuinely-undecomposed epics; one loop iteration dispatches exactly one decompose; subsequent iterations handle the remaining two.
7. TestWorkIdle_OverrideFlagSuppressesDecompose: --no-auto-epic-decompose set; loop does NOT dispatch even when all preconditions pass; bead is surfaced as gated_by_budget_or_cooldown with detail "no-auto-epic-decompose".
8. TestWorkIdle_NoSuccessFallsThroughToSleep: only beads with non-remediable diagnoses present; loop falls through to existing sleepOrWake path, emits loop.idle, behavior matches today.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1 green.
10. cd cli && go test ./... green.
11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315 today reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail, emits loop.idle, and either returns (--once / drain) or sleeps (--watch). It never diagnoses or auto-remediates. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation requires the loop to diagnose every non-ready bead, fire matching remediators, and re-scan without sleeping when at least one remediation succeeded.

ROOT CAUSE
No diagnose+dispatch logic exists at the insertion point.

PROPOSED FIX
Inside execute_bead_loop.go, after ReadyExecutionBreakdown populates breakdown (~line 1265) and before applyStop/sleepOrWake (~line 1271), insert the idle-path auto-remediation block.

Pseudo-flow (translate to Go):
1. Build childIndex, openChildIndex, depIndex from the store (these may already be computed inside ReadyExecutionBreakdown; expose them or rebuild as needed).
2. Walk breakdown.Epics ∪ breakdown.DepWaiting ∪ breakdown.OperatorAttention. For each bead, call bead.Diagnose(...) (from cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go).
3. Switch on the BlockReasonCode:
   - superseded_pending_close: queue a no-op (the cascade happens automatically inside any subsequent Close(); to actively trigger it on a known X, call store.Close on the SUPERSEDER Y if it's somehow still open in this branch — typically not; if X is the bead and Y is already closed but the cascade never ran, call a new store.RunSupersededCascade(X, Y) helper that runs the same scope-checked cascade out of band).
   - closure_candidate_misclassified: this should disappear once the RC-1 classifier fix lands; if it still appears, log and surface (defensive).
   - dead_intermediate_all_children_closed: trigger the existing closure-evaluation path against the bead.
   - dead_intermediate_open_children_pending OR epic_of_epic: skip (recursion via children handles it).
   - genuinely_needs_decomposition: queue an AutoDecomposeEpic call. At most ONE auto-decompose dispatch per loop iteration (track a counter). Skip if --no-auto-epic-decompose set.
   - dep_blocked_by, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis: no idle-path action; will surface via ddx work focus.
   - stale_graph_index: trigger an index rebuild and re-diagnose next loop.
4. Execute queued remediations sequentially. Track count of successes.
5. If any remediation succeeded, emit loop.auto_remediated event with {remediation_counts: {...}, total_attempted, total_succeeded}, clearTransientCandidateSkips, and `continue` (re-scan without entering sleepOrWake).
6. If none succeeded, fall through to the existing emit("loop.idle", ...) + sleepOrWake path.

Add operator override flags to cli/cmd/work.go: --no-auto-supersede-close, --no-auto-epic-decompose, --no-auto-closure-reclassify. Plumb them through to the work loop via runtime config (mirror how existing --max-recovery-cost flows in).

Add config key work.autoRemediations: { autoSupersedeClose: bool, autoEpicDecompose: bool, autoClosureReclassify: bool } with defaults of true. Flags override config; per-project config overrides defaults.

Reuse existing cost budget threading: AutoDecomposeEpic counts against --max-recovery-cost just like the existing post-TooLarge decomposer does. Honor SmartRetryCooldown / SetExecutionCooldown precedent for per-bead cooldown after failed auto-decompose (already implemented in the auto_decompose_epic bead).

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the diagnoser, RC-1 fix, RC-2 cascade, RC-3 walk-up, RC-6 handler — those are separate beads this one depends on.
- Operator surface changes (ddx work focus, ddx work plan) — separate beads.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. Depends on ddx-79e66725 (diagnose), ddx-e9f3adaf (RC-1 lifecycle), ddx-04e89823 (RC-2 cascade), ddx-f500c75b (RC-3 walk-up), ddx-7b88e3a7 (RC-6 auto-decompose handler).

description:
PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

Operators who run one Claude Code/Codex install per machine need a `--global` surface so skills/personas install once into the machine-wide DDx tree and are discoverable from every project without per-project setup. Today the global install and update paths must write to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and link into the home-tier agent skill dirs, using a separate global state file so they never touch a project's install.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx install` carries the `--global` flag at cli/cmd/install.go:49 and routes through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184) and `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202). For global=true, resolveInstallDirs returns installRoot=`ddxroot.GlobalDir()`, agentSkillsDir=`~/.agents/skills`, claudeSkillsDir=`~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-193); selectInstallState returns `registry.LoadGlobalState`/`SaveGlobalState` (install.go:206-208). Targets are remapped by `adjustInstallTargets`/`remapSkillTargets` (install.go:215,235). Global installs skip the project auto-commit (install.go:174).
- `ddx update --global` routes through `performGlobalUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:300), which operates exclusively on `registry.LoadGlobalState()`/`ddxroot.GlobalDir()` (update.go:301,337,345) and never touches the project tree; the flag is parsed at update.go:395 and dispatched at update.go:66.
- The `--global` flag must also be present on `ddx plugin list`/`ddx plugin show` so query surfaces can target the global tier.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Verify `ddx install <name> --global --silent` writes the plugin under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>, recording the result in the global state file (not the project state).
2. Verify `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and leaves any project install untouched.
3. Add/confirm `--global` on `ddx plugin list` and `ddx plugin show`.
4. Document the global install location in library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works".
5. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09) must continue to scope skill validation to staged skill files regardless of install location.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).
- Project (non-global) install behavior and init/doctor wiring (sibling children).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global install routes through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.
title: install/update: --global surface installs to XDG global tree + agent-tier skill links

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-eea2dfca is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead only adds the Niflheim observation as regression coverage and decision/audit wiring.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|PreClaim|RetryEscalation|PreClaimDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-937de9fc is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 5 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.
- Spec updates (ADR/skill-topology + CLAUDE.md Persona System) landed in this bead's commit.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and: part 5

description:
PROBLEM
The feature is only credible if an automated isolated test proves server-managed worker cleanup leaves no ddx work, claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants behind. Unit tests are necessary but not sufficient for process-tree cleanup.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214 Stop and :1423 reapWorker are process-cleanup entry points, but existing tests do not run a full server plus managed worker plus fake agent child tree in an isolated environment and assert post-cleanup process state. docs/helix/06-iterate/server-managed-workers-plan-2026-06-13.md Testing Strategy requires a Docker-enabled no-leak scenario.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a Docker-backed integration test or scripts/integration/server-managed-workers-docker.sh. Build DDx from current source in the container, create a fixture repo with a deterministic script/fake harness, put fake claude and codex binaries first on PATH, start ddx server, set desired_count=1 through server API or ddx worker CLI, wait for fake children, then exercise explicit stop, watchdog reap, restart, and server shutdown. Assert no managed ddx work, fake claude/codex, or descendant shell/sleep process remains after each cleanup path.

NON-SCOPE
Do not require real Claude/Codex credentials. Do not rely on host tmux or host process state. Do not make the test mandatory on developer machines without Docker; skip clearly there, but provide a CI/release command that runs when Docker is available.
title: integration: prove server-managed workers leave no process leaks in Docker

acceptance: 1. cli/internal/bead/merge.go strips dependency edges whose target is absent from the merged set (active union archive) after union; covered by a new merge test that feeds a bead with a dangling dep and asserts the merged output has it removed. 2. cli/internal/bead/import.go strips (not just warns about) dangling deps; test asserts the imported bead has no dangling dep and the reported count is accurate. 3. detectPrefix never returns a prefix that fails ValidateID (no leading '.', no '/'); test feeds a worktree-style workingDir and asserts the prefix falls back to DefaultPrefix. 4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... green. 5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM: ddx accumulated ~132 phantom dependency edges (dependencies[].depends_on_id pointing to bead IDs absent from beads.jsonl AND beads-archive.jsonl), which permanently blocked ~29 beads from readiness and stalled the live queue (readyFiltered/classifyLifecycleQueue treat a missing-target dep as unsatisfied forever). The REPAIR + one write-path guard already landed (merge to main: ddx bead doctor gained a 'dangling_dep' finding that prunes such edges archive-aware; Store.updateBead now rejects NEWLY-introduced dangling edges). This bead closes the REMAINING source paths so phantoms stop appearing.

ROOT CAUSES still open (from investigation):
1. cli/internal/bead/merge.go MergeTrackerConflictJSONL set-unions the dependencies list via mergeObjectLists(dependencyMergeKey,...) with NO referential-integrity check (merge.go:~162). A parent's dep edges merge in while child records on a dropped branch do not -> dangling. PRIMARY accumulation path for ddx-internal merges (ddx bead import / sync).
2. cli/internal/bead/import.go:~281 detects dangling deps but only WARNS ('dangling dep (skipped)') and appends the bead WITH the dangling dep intact. The '(skipped)' wording is false.
3. cli/internal/bead/store.go detectPrefix (~:3070-3087) returns filepath.Base(workingDir)/filepath.Base(cwd) when 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' is unavailable; run inside an execute-bead worktree this yields a prefix like '.execute-bead-wt-...', producing malformed child IDs (one such ID is live in the tracker). ValidateID should have rejected a leading '.', so these bypass Create/GenID validation via raw WriteAll/import/merge.

PROPOSED FIX:
- merge.go: after the union, drop any dependency edge whose depends_on_id is not present in the merged bead set UNION the archive sibling (mirror doctor.go loadArchiveBeadIDs). Add a referential-integrity pass in MergeTrackerConflictJSONL (or ValidateTrackerJSONLUnique).
- import.go: actually strip dangling deps (filter them out of the bead's Dependencies) and report count; keep the warning but make it true.
- detectPrefix: reject a derived prefix that fails the ValidateID charset (or contains '.'/'/'); fall back to DefaultPrefix. Optionally validate GenID output against ValidateID before returning.
NON-SCOPE: the doctor 'dangling_dep' finding + updateBead guard are already landed; do not redo them. Do not change readiness semantics here (separate decision).

CONTEXT: the durable cross-machine convergence works today because doctor --fix bumps updated_at and the supervisor union resolver keeps newest-updated_at, so pruned records propagate. This bead is about stopping NEW phantoms at the ddx write/merge/import layer.
title: Close the remaining phantom-dep write paths: merge union, import, and worktree-prefix ID contamination

2. TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active (in-tree project .ddx/plugins vs convention-mode XDG project plugins).

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

`ddx update` targets the project path unconditionally with no `--global` surface, `ddx doctor` does not report the global vs project install layers, and the install-topology decision is undocumented.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/update.go targets ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and has no `--global` flag and no in-tree-vs-convention branch.
- ddx doctor has no lines reporting the global tier (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) or the project tier (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) install status.
- No ADR or CLAUDE.md text records the install-to-agent vs install-in-project topology and precedence.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` to `ddx update`. When set, update only the global plugin tree under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>; do not touch the project tree.
2. Without `--global`, `ddx update` updates the project install in whichever mode is active (in-tree <project>/.ddx/plugins or convention-mode XDG project plugins).
3. `ddx doctor` reports both layers: a "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" line and a "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global" line, using the tier-resolution info exposed by the registry bead.
4. Land the spec/docs updates: file-or-update the skill-install-topology ADR (ADR-025 or the equivalent skill-topology record), document install locations in library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works", document agent-tier paths in library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md if that file exists, and document the install precedence in the CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section.

NON-SCOPE
- install / init `--global` flags (sibling beads).
- The runtime precedence implementation (registry bead).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir + tier reporting) — update and doctor consume it.
- install sibling bead — doctor's project-tier reporting assumes the install layout it produces.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.
- ddx-06cbaa90 spec updates (this bead's spec edits are in addition to those).
- ADR-025-or-equivalent (skill install topology).
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" precedence note.
title: update + doctor + specs: --global update, mode-respecting update, two-layer doctor report, and spec/docs updates

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 6 archive/interchange methods (`Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile`). Per TD-027 §6 archive belongs on `BeadArchive` interface; interchange on `BeadInterchangeReader`/`BeadInterchangeWriter`. All take ctx per AC #1. These are signature-only changes — no helper migration needed; the methods stay as direct storage primitives.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go`, `cli/internal/bead/archive.go`, `cli/internal/bead/import.go` define these 6 methods without ctx. Callers: `cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go` (export/import subcommands).

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update `*Store.Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile` to take `ctx context.Context` first.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadArchive = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers (`cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go`).

# NON-SCOPE
- Archive policy (TD-027 §16) — not modified.
- Attachment storage layout (TD-027 §17) — not modified.
- bd/br interchange shape (TD-027 §20) — wire format unchanged.
- Migration tooling (`MigrateLifecycle`/`MigrateFromHelix`/etc.) — separate concern, handled in MIG bead.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. The 6 methods take ctx as first parameter. Compile-time assertions for `BeadArchive`, `BeadInterchangeReader`, `BeadInterchangeWriter` compile.
2. Callers thread ctx. Existing archive/import/export tests pass.
3. `ddx bead archive`, `ddx bead export`, `ddx bead import` CLI commands continue to work end-to-end. Test: `TestBeadArchiveCommand_RoundTrip`, `TestBeadExportImport_RoundTripsAllFields`.
4. Schema round-trip preserved (per TD-027 §20 — bd/br interchange compatibility). Test: `TestBead_ExportImportRoundTrip_PreservesExtras`.
5. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:archive, area:interchange, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784.
title: A: Archive + Interchange — add ctx, satisfy BeadArchive/InterchangeR/W; signature-only (TD-027 §6)

acceptance:
1. New Vitest `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/design-tokens.test.ts`: the resolved Tailwind theme contains NO generic values (no `#8e35a3`, no `#4878c6`, no `#f9fafb`/`#ffffff` surface) and `accent-lever === '#3B5B7A'`; regenerating `design-tokens.json` from DESIGN.md keeps these.
2. Inter is actually LOADED: a Playwright assertion that `document.fonts` includes Inter (not merely declared) and `getComputedStyle(h1).fontFamily` resolves to the loaded family; re-baseline `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` snapshots.
3. The `semanticColors` alias layer is retained (class names unchanged); design:export remains the source of truth.
4. cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test && bun run test:e2e green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM: Two issues mask the intended design system (`.stitch/DESIGN.md`: warm-paper brutalist). (1) `cli/internal/server/frontend/design-tokens.json` is the STALE generic Stitch export (`tertiary:#8e35a3` purple, `surface:#f9fafb`, Inter for all roles) — its warm palette only reaches the app because `cli/internal/server/frontend/tailwind.config.js:8-37` hardcodes `semanticColors` that override the JSON. So `bun run design:export` regenerates GENERIC values; a reader cannot tell which file is authoritative. (2) The typeface (`Inter`, tailwind.config.js:39-46) is SPECIFIED but never LOADED (no @font-face/@fontsource/Google Fonts anywhere) -> renders the OS system-sans fallback.

ROOT CAUSE: the design:export pipeline emits the generic default token DATA (not DESIGN.md's warm palette); and no font asset is imported. Per codex: `semanticColors` is the load-bearing alias layer (KEEP it) — only the generated token DATA is stale. Operator decision: load Inter (do NOT introduce a new display font — that would be a spec change).

PROPOSED FIX: Regenerate `design-tokens.json` from `.stitch/DESIGN.md` (warm palette: #3B5B7A lever-steel, #F4EFE6 cream, etc.) so the export pipeline is the source of truth and carries the correct values; KEEP the `semanticColors` alias layer in tailwind.config.js (do not delete it; ensure it reads the regenerated tokens rather than shadowing stale ones). Add Inter via `@fontsource-variable/inter` (`bun add`), import in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/app.css`, so all non-mono roles render Inter instead of system fallback.

NON-SCOPE: introducing a distinctive display font (operator chose Inter-only); off-palette utility migration (separate bead); deleting the semanticColors layer.

description:
# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:storage, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027, reliability

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: all per-concern beads must complete before this bead can execute:
- ddx-bca628fa (L)
- ddx-e1c743d3 (C)
- ddx-f39b41b3 (E)
- ddx-aed7c7ab (X)
- ddx-bc07270f (Q)
- ddx-3af1c1a6 (D)
- ddx-71d3a2de (A)
- ddx-0ab16765 (MIG)

Plus required infrastructure:
- ddx-9c5bca8f (AxonStore — its concrete type is also relocated)
- ddx-29f02cf4 (config factory — factory.go takes over construction)
- ddx-900a8d38 (WatcherHub factory — must be migrated to BeadReader factory before lockdown)

# What this earns

**Physically impossible bypass enforced by the Go compiler.** Any future caller in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, etc. that tries to import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` fails to build. The contract becomes: callers see only the public `Backend` interface (and sub-interfaces, Operations, sentinels, IDGenerator, factory); they cannot reach the concrete type.

description:
PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

Without `--global`, `ddx install`/`ddx update` must target the active project install location, which differs by mode: in-tree mode (a `<project>/.ddx/` exists) writes under `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`; convention mode (no `.ddx/`) writes under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/`. In both modes the project-scoped agent skill dirs (`<project>/.claude/skills/` and `<project>/.agents/skills/`) must link into whichever plugins/ path the mode resolves to.

ROOT CAUSE
- For global=false, `resolveInstallDirs` (cli/cmd/install.go:184) returns installRoot=`ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs under `f.WorkingDir/.agents/skills` and `f.WorkingDir/.claude/skills` (install.go:195-197). `ddxroot.Path()` is what selects the in-tree vs convention plugins root, so the install root automatically follows the mode.
- `ddx update` (no flag) must update the project install in whichever mode is active; the non-global path in update.go (dispatched when `opts.Global` is false, update.go:66) operates on the project working dir's resolved root.
- Project installs auto-commit trackable project changes (install.go:174-176) — global installs do not.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Verify `ddx install <name>` (no --global) in in-tree mode installs to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and links under `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`.
2. Verify `ddx install <name>` (no --global) in convention mode installs to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/` and the project-tier links point into that XDG plugins/ path.
3. Verify `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active and does not touch the global tree.
4. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09) must continue to scope skill validation to staged skill files regardless of mode.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).
- The `--global` install/update surface (sibling child).
- init/doctor wiring (sibling child).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project install root is computed by `ddxroot.Path()`, which encodes the in-tree vs convention decision.
title: install/update: project install honors in-tree vs convention mode + project-tier skill links

README.md gains a pre-claim intake section that (a) names the pre-claim intake contract and the readiness_checks payload shape (referencing the shared schema), (b) tabulates the failure-class mapping — warn-only vs operator-attention vs hard error — including the consecutive-fingerprint escalation threshold and the rolling claim-success-rate warning, and (c) gives an operator a step-by-step way to diagnose silent-idle on a full queue (agent-loop JSONL location, fingerprint meaning, worker-status query).

description:
PROBLEM
The pre-claim intake contract and its failure-class behavior are undocumented for operators. When a worker silently idles on a full queue (the ddx-f9ddaa68 symptom), an operator has no written guidance on what pre-claim intake is, how warn vs operator-attention vs hard-error outcomes differ, or how to diagnose silent-idle. The 'suggested_action' text in warn events ('check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry') is itself misleading when the fault is in DDx's own decoder.

CONTEXT / WHERE
The operator-facing README is /Users/erik/Projects/.../README.md (repo root; there is no cli/README.md). Pre-claim intake mechanics live in cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go and readiness_decision.go.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a README section documenting:
  1. The pre-claim intake contract: what it evaluates before claim and the readiness_checks payload shape (point at the shared schema from ddx-c5d485c9).
  2. The failure-class mapping: warn-only (skip + keep polling) vs operator-attention (escalate + stop) vs hard error — including the consecutive-fingerprint escalation threshold (ddx-d67f124e) and the rolling claim-success-rate warning (ddx-977e1a7f).
  3. How operators diagnose silent-idle: where the agent-loop JSONL lives, what the pre_claim_intake.warn fingerprint means, and how to query worker status.

DEPENDENCY
Depends on ddx-606fe854, ddx-d67f124e, ddx-977e1a7f, ddx-c5d485c9 — document the behavior those children implement.

NON-SCOPE
No code changes; README (and, if needed, a linked docs/ page) only.

description:
PROBLEM
The work/work CLI path still extracts each flag into local variables and threads those values through bespoke inline logic. The root bead requires a single cobra-to-spec conversion so execution fields are not re-maintained at every boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 reads ProjectRoot/from/routing/review/cost/timeout/power flags into local variables, then cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1759-1777 manually projects a subset into config.CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadRuntime. Control-plane flags are mixed with execution fields in the same RunE body.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd, treatPassthroughAsOpaque) returning (executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec, executeloop.DispatchOptions, error).
- Make runAgentExecuteLoopImpl call parseExecuteLoopSpec once, then use the spec for inline execution inputs and the dispatch options for --json/--local behavior.
- Keep current ADR-022 behavior: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1509-1511 says work always runs inline and --local is a deprecated no-op. If a live HTTP submit helper still exists, make it accept and marshal ExecuteLoopSpec directly; otherwise do not resurrect server-submit mode.
- Preserve ddx try behavior: tryTargetBeadID still forces Once true and FromRev still reaches ExecuteBeadRuntime.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change route validation semantics or auto-infer tier behavior.
- Do not change server REST/GraphQL handlers; separate children own those transports.
- Do not reshape LabelFilter.

acceptance:
1. TP-021 contains a "Durable-audit commit collision-safety contract" paragraph stating lock-timeout and signal:killed/command-deadline are transient (not operator-attention), the commit is idempotent/resumable, and concurrent workers back off rather than kill.
2. TP-021 Fast Chaos list names the regression test shipped by B2.
3. FEAT-010 states transient durable-audit-commit contention is not an operator-attention condition while non-transient git failure still is.

description:
PROBLEM: TP-021:44-47 frames the .ddx/.git-tracker.lock 30s only as a PERFORMANCE contract and TP-021:38 names the durable-audit window without a collision-safety contract; FEAT-010's operator-attention discussion (around FEAT-010:294, :322, :797) never carves out transient git contention. The B1 (ddx-83361480) / B2 (ddx-e8bf0695) behavior must be pinned in the specs so it does not regress.

ROOT CAUSE: missing contract text — TP-021:38, TP-021:44-47, FEAT-010:322.

PROPOSED FIX: (1) Extend TP-021's durable-audit window bullet (TP-021:38) and append a "Durable-audit commit collision-safety contract" paragraph after TP-021:47 stating: lock-acquire timeout AND a git-subprocess SIGKILL/context-deadline ("signal: killed") on any status/add/commit step MUST be classified transient and retried (never operator_attention); the commit MUST be idempotent/resumable (re-run completes with no manual git reset); concurrent workers serialize on the durable-audit critical section with a bounded wait that BACKS OFF and returns a transient error rather than killing the git child; genuine non-transient failure (permissions, ENOSPC, corruption) still surfaces operator attention. (2) Add the regression test name shipped by B2 to TP-021 Fast Chaos list (after TP-021:78). (3) Add the one-sentence transient-contention carve-out to FEAT-010 near :322.

NON-SCOPE: code changes (B1/B2); editing the gitlock package contract (unchanged — gitlock.IsIndexLockError is correct; the gap is the agent-layer classifier + deadline policy).
IN-SCOPE FILES: docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-021*.md, docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md.

description:
PROBLEM

Cancelling a long-running 'ddx work' with Ctrl-C (SIGINT) prints noisy
error spam after the graceful-shutdown lines. Observed output:

    Cancel received, shutting down gracefully
    project: /home/erik/Projects/niflheim
    worker exited: stopped by signal
    attempts: 0  |  closed: 0  |  changed: 0  |  already-satisfied: 0  |  failures: 0
    Error: context canceled
    Usage:
      ddx work [flags]
      ddx work [command]

    Examples:
      # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
      ddx work
      ... (full usage/examples dump)

The "Cancel received..." line and the terminal summary are fine. The
"Error: context canceled" line AND the entire "Usage:"/"Examples:" block
are spam. A clean Ctrl-C of a long-running command should exit quietly
(exit code 130), not print a raw error and the help text.

ROOT CAUSE

There are two compounding defects, both on the 'ddx work' RunE -> Cobra path:

1. The execute loop returns the raw context.Canceled error up to RunE.
   cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:662-666: after worker.Run returns with
   err wrapping context.Canceled, line 662-664 writes the terminal
   summary, then line 665-666 does `if err != nil { return err }`,
   returning context.Canceled to runWork.
   - runWork (cli/cmd/work.go:134-157) returns that error unchanged via
     `return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")` at work.go:156.

2. The 'work' command does NOT set SilenceUsage/SilenceErrors, and neither
   does the root command, so Cobra's ExecuteContext prints both the raw
   error and the usage block before the error can be mapped to a clean exit.
   - newWorkCommand (cli/cmd/work.go:22-127) builds the cobra.Command at
     work.go:23-73 with NO SilenceUsage/SilenceErrors field (contrast
     cli/cmd/try.go:46 `SilenceUsage: true` and cli/cmd/run.go:26
     `SilenceUsage: true`, which is why ddx try / ddx run do NOT dump usage).
   - The root command, NewRootCommand (cli/cmd/command_factory.go:136-226,
     command spec at 143-168), also sets neither field.
   - Because SilenceErrors is unset, Cobra prints "Error: context canceled".
     Because SilenceUsage is unset and context.Canceled is a runtime
     (non-arg-parse) error, Cobra also dumps the full Usage/Examples block.

3. The clean-exit mapping happens too late to suppress the spam.
   cli/cmd/root.go:52-56: executeRootCommand returns, and Execute maps a
   context.Canceled error to NewExitError(130, "") so cli/main.go:33-38
   prints nothing and exits 130. But this mapping runs AFTER
   rootCmd.ExecuteContext (cli/cmd/root_signal.go:47) has already printed
   the "Error:" line and the Usage block. So exit code 130 is correct, but
   the spam is already on stderr.

WORKING REFERENCE: ddx try (cli/cmd/try.go:46) and ddx run
(cli/cmd/run.go:26) set SilenceUsage:true on their command structs, so a
runtime error never dumps usage for those commands. ddx work just needs
the same treatment plus suppression of the raw context.Canceled error.

PROPOSED FIX

- cli/cmd/work.go:23-73  add `SilenceUsage: true` (and `SilenceErrors: true`)
  to the cobra.Command literal in newWorkCommand, mirroring try.go:46 /
  run.go:26, so a runtime error from runWork never dumps the usage block.
- cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:662-666  when err wraps context.Canceled
  (operator SIGINT), do NOT return the raw error up to RunE after the
  terminal summary has been written; return nil (or a sentinel that
  main.go maps to exit 130) so Cobra prints no "Error: context canceled"
  line. DeadlineExceeded / other errors must still propagate as today.
  Preserve the existing exit-code-130 behavior: if you return nil here,
  ensure SIGINT still yields exit 130 (e.g. keep the root.go:52-56
  context.Canceled -> NewExitError(130,"") mapping reachable, or return an
  ExitError(130,"") sentinel from this layer). Do not regress the exit code.
- Keep the "Cancel received, shutting down gracefully" line and the
  "worker exited: stopped by signal" terminal summary; only the
  "Error: context canceled" line and the Usage/Examples dump are removed.

NON-SCOPE

- ddx try and ddx run already set SilenceUsage (try.go:46, run.go:26); do
  not touch them beyond what is needed to share a helper if you choose to.
- Do not change the graceful-shutdown announcer (cli/cmd/root_signal.go)
  or the "stopped by signal" summary text (cli/cmd/work.go:260-261).
- Do not alter DeadlineExceeded handling or non-signal error propagation.
title: ddx work: exit cleanly on Ctrl-C without printing 'context canceled' + usage dump

3. TestDiagnose covers the priority-match behavior: a bead matching both superseded_pending_close and dep_blocked_by reports superseded_pending_close (first match in FEAT-010 order).
4. The function is pure: no calls to store.Get, no file I/O, no global state mutation. Verified by code reading.

6. cd cli && go test ./... green (no regressions in other packages).

description:
PROBLEM
ddx work's idle path (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315) reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail and then sleeps or exits. There is no per-bead diagnosis of WHY each non-ready bead is non-ready, so the loop cannot fire targeted auto-remediations. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires a closed reason taxonomy with one code per non-ready bead.

ROOT CAUSE
No diagnoser exists. ReadyExecutionBreakdown returns bucket lists (Epics, EpicClosureCandidates, DepWaiting, OperatorAttention) but does not annotate each entry with why-it-is-blocked detail at a granularity the work loop can switch on.

PROPOSED FIX
Add cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go with a pure function Diagnose(b Bead, childIndex map[string][]Bead, openChildIndex map[string][]Bead, depIndex map[string][]Dependency) BlockReason. BlockReason is a typed struct: { Code BlockReasonCode; Detail string; CitedIDs []string }. BlockReasonCode is a typed-string enum with exactly the codes listed in FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation (superseded_pending_close, closure_candidate_misclassified, dead_intermediate_all_children_closed, dead_intermediate_open_children_pending, epic_of_epic, dep_blocked_by, genuinely_needs_decomposition, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, stale_graph_index, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis). The function is pure — no I/O — and takes pre-built indexes so it can be called from both the loop and the CLI.

Detection rules per code are specified in the FEAT-010 table. For superseded_pending_close, read the bead's superseded-by field OR a superseded-by:<Y> label, then verify Y is closed in the index. For closure_candidate_misclassified, check IssueType==epic AND len(openChildIndex[b.ID])==0 AND len(childIndex[b.ID])>0. For genuinely_needs_decomposition, check IssueType==epic AND len(childIndex[b.ID])==0 AND no manual-hold/no-auto-decompose/container label. For dep_blocked_by, cite every open dep's ID and status. Match codes in the order they appear in the FEAT-010 table; first match wins.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring Diagnose into the work loop (separate bead).
- Inventing new reason codes beyond the FEAT-010 taxonomy.
- Mutations of any kind.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. No deps.
title: diagnose: per-bead idle-scan reason classifier

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
`ddx update` must mirror the install topology: `--global` updates only the machine-wide plugin tree under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/`, while the no-flag form updates the active project install in whichever mode (in-tree or convention) is currently in effect, leaving the other tier untouched.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/update.go:58 `runUpdate` branches on `--global` (comment at update.go:65) to cli/cmd/update.go:300 `performGlobalUpdate`, otherwise to cli/cmd/update.go:204 `performUpdate(workingDir, opts)` which operates on `ddxroot.Path`-resolved targets and `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets` (update.go:122).
- Without locked-in tests, a `--global` update could touch the project tree (or a project update could touch global), violating tier isolation.

PROPOSED FIX
- Ensure `ddx update --global` writes only under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/` and never mutates any project plugins/ dir.
- Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install resolved by `ddxroot.Path()` — `<project>/.ddx/plugins/` in in-tree mode, the XDG `projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/` dir in convention mode — and does not mutate the global tree.
- Tests sandbox `XDG_DATA_HOME`/`HOME`, seed both tiers, run the command, and assert mtimes/content changed in exactly one tier.

NON-SCOPE
- Install/init surfaces — sibling beads.
- doctor reporting and spec/docs updates — sibling bead.
- Removing the per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) for `ddxroot.Path()` / `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.
- Sibling install beads (global + project install must exist to seed update targets).
title: update: `ddx update --global` updates global tree only; `ddx update` is mode-aware

description:
PROBLEM
Genuinely undecomposed epics (IssueType==epic, totalChildCount==0, no closure-pending state) need automated decomposition into child task beads so ddx work can drain them. Today the only decomposition path is operator-invoked (bead-breakdown skill) or post-TooLarge recovery (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go). FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires the work loop to auto-dispatch decomposition for this specific case (RC-6) with strict preconditions to avoid the v1-plan duplicate-child bug.

ROOT CAUSE
No handler exists that bridges the diagnoser's genuinely_needs_decomposition reason code to runPreClaimDecomposer (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:48). The existing post-TooLarge hook (NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook at recovery_decompose.go:107) gates only on TooLarge failure class, not on idle-scan diagnosis.

PROPOSED FIX
Add cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go exporting AutoDecomposeEpic(ctx, store, runner, rcfg, projectRoot, beadID) (DecomposeResult, error). It calls runPreClaimDecomposer (reused unchanged) after asserting every precondition. Preconditions (each must hold; first failing precondition produces a typed error AND increments the per-bead auto_decompose_attempted event count):
- store.Get(beadID).IssueType == "epic"
- totalChildCount(beadID) == 0 (use the same index ReadyExecutionBreakdown uses)
- bead is not currently in the EpicClosureCandidate bucket
- bead has no open dep_blocked_by or closed_or_missing_parent diagnoses (call the new Diagnose function on the bead; if it returns either, return ErrDepsBlockDecompose)
- bead carries no label in {manual-hold, no-auto-decompose, container}
- bead description is non-empty AND contains PROBLEM AND PROPOSED FIX sections AND has at least one acceptance criterion AND has at least one label matching ^(spec|area):
- per-bead event count of auto_decompose_attempted < 3
- bead has no active execution cooldown
- (operator override --no-auto-epic-decompose checked at the caller, not here)

On success: parent epic's execution_eligible is set to false (mirrors recovery_decompose.go behavior), child beads are created via store.Create, the auto_decompose_attempted event is emitted with body {child_ids: [...], cost_usd: N}.

On failure: SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, now+15m, "auto_decompose_failed", reason, baseRev), emit auto_decompose_failed event. On the 3rd cumulative attempt failure also emit auto_remediation_exhausted so the diagnoser surfaces it.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring this into the work loop (separate bead).
- Changing the runPreClaimDecomposer prompt (reused as-is).
- Surfacing the operator override flag (work-loop bead handles flag plumbing).

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. Depends on ddx-79e66725 (diagnose function) and ddx-e9f3adaf (RC-1 lifecycle fix — the closure-bucket check needs the correct classifier).
title: agent: auto_decompose_epic — wrap runPreClaimDecomposer for genuinely-undecomposed epics

description:
PROBLEM (child of ddx-937de9fc)
Non-global ddx install / ddx update assume an in-tree .ddx/. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the plugin must land under the XDG project dir (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/) with project-tier skill links pointing into it. Today install writes to the literal relative .ddx/plugins path.

ROOT CAUSE
- registry.InstallPackage(pkg, f.WorkingDir) and the default target defaultPackageRootTarget (installer.go:128) build a project-relative '.ddx/plugins/<name>' string; they do not route through ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) which already resolves in-tree vs convention (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75).
- update.go performUpdate (update.go:194) and commitPluginChanges (install.go:210) likewise assume .ddx/ in-tree.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Resolve the project plugin root through ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name> for the non-global path so convention mode lands under the XDG project dir and in-tree mode stays under <project>/.ddx/plugins/.
2. Create project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name> pointing into whichever plugins root resolved.
3. ddx update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.

NON-SCOPE: --global (ddx-aedb009d); init behavior; runtime precedence (ddx-18e55cd2).

DEPS: ddx-18e55cd2.
title: install/update: project install honors in-tree vs convention mode via ddxroot.Path()

description:
PROBLEM
Once work supports initial and repair cycles, operators need a durable append-only trace for every implementation and repair cycle. Today the loop emits bead.result and review events, but there is no single cycle trace schema that connects cycle_index, attempt_id, implementer route facts, review group slots, review verdicts, and final decision.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:31-67 defines the try.Report shape without cycle_index, review_group_id, reviewer slot, or final cycle decision fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:9-46 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:49-86 copy report fields between legacy and try shapes, so any new trace fields must be wired through both directions while the compatibility layer exists.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1339-1349 emits bead.result without cycle trace fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:56-94 builds review_group_id and reviewer_index slot data, but that data remains review-local rather than being attached to a durable cycle trace.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:200-227 records the reduced review verdict and closes on APPROVE without appending a unified initial/repair cycle record.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an append-only execution cycle trace data shape for initial implementation cycles and review repair cycles.
- Required fields: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, review result, and final decision.
- Persist the trace in execution evidence and/or bead events using an append-only format that can survive retries and process restarts.
- Wire the trace through the try.Report/legacy ExecuteBeadReport adapter while both shapes exist.
- Cover both the first implementation cycle and the bounded repair cycle introduced by ddx-ece97605.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement repair-cycle scheduling; this bead consumes the repair behavior from ddx-ece97605.
- Do not build frontend UI for cycle traces.
- Do not prune, compact, or rewrite older execution evidence.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-ece97605 so the trace test can cover both initial and repair cycles.
title: evidence: record append-only implementation and repair cycle trace

description:
PROBLEM
Plan/work review can hang on Claude even when trivial Claude prompts work. A stalled review must not block implementation indefinitely or leave unmanaged claude processes behind.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The server-managed worker plan records four bounded Claude review attempts on 2026-06-13 that produced no output before timeout, while a trivial sonnet prompt returned. The same failure recurred after ddx was installed at afc8b6408 using timeout 180s claude -p --model sonnet --permission-mode plan against docs/helix/06-iterate/server-managed-workers-plan-2026-06-13.md. Existing worker cleanup in cli/internal/server/workers.go:1282-1286 and :1453-1457 only kills known worker process groups; review subprocesses launched outside that supervision are not represented as managed worker children.

PROPOSED FIX
Route review dispatches that can be part of ddx work/review gates through the same bounded supervisor used for managed workers. Every review attempt must have a wall-clock deadline, phase heartbeat/progress evidence, process-group ownership or child registration, and an operator_attention/review-error event on timeout. Timeout cleanup must kill the review process tree and leave no claude/codex descendants owned by that attempt.

NON-SCOPE
Do not make real Claude success required for unit tests. Do not change model routing names in this bead. Do not kill unrelated interactive Claude/Codex sessions.
title: agent: bound review dispatches and clean hung Claude processes

description:
PROBLEM (captured live): Two `ddx work --watch` workers on the same checkout /Users/erik/Projects/ddx collided. Worker 1 ran ddx-69a9ecd3 to completion (closed, 1h11m) but its durable-audit commit was SIGKILLed and the worker exited "operator attention required", leaving .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, .ddx/attachments dirty. Memory was healthy (31Gi free) — NOT OOM. Error: `commit durable audit outputs: committing durable audit outputs: : signal: killed`.

ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:88 wraps the WHOLE commit body (status/add/diff/commit) in context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second); that ctx flows to exec.CommandContext (cli/internal/git/exec.go:38). While the git commit was blocked behind the concurrent worker's hold on the shared main-git lock (.ddx/.git-tracker.lock via withTrackerLock, tracker_lock.go:133-152) / .git/index.lock, the 30s deadline fired and Go sent SIGKILL to git -> CombinedOutput() returns empty output + *exec.ExitError("signal: killed") (durable_audit.go:125-127). The classifier isTransientGitContention (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1026-1041) matches index.lock / "unable to write new index file" / "cannot lock ref" / "unable to update the ref" but NOT "signal: killed"/context-deadline, so finalizeDurableAuditOrStop (execute_bead_loop.go:1311-1336) sets OperatorAttention and exits. The commit is ALREADY idempotent on re-entry (durable_audit.go:98-122), so a retry would succeed — only the misclassification-driven EXIT blocks the idempotent re-run.

PROPOSED FIX: Make isTransientGitContention (execute_bead_loop.go:1026) a SUPERSET that returns true for: (a) errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) and substrings "signal: killed" and "context deadline exceeded" (the SIGKILL/deadline class — defense-in-depth since the exec.ExitError from a ctx kill may not retrievably wrap context.DeadlineExceeded), AND (b) the "tracker lock timeout (...)" string from tracker_lock.go:283 (the lock-acquire-wait-exhausted class). This SUPERSEDES ddx-2189a07f (which adds only the tracker-lock-timeout string and is currently wedging on a routing issue) — folding both transient forms into one coherent classifier change avoids two beads racing to edit the same function. Keep all existing matches.

NON-SCOPE: changing the 30s deadline value or decoupling it from the lock wait (that is the sibling bead B2); worktree-leak cleanup (ddx-274943fc); classifier call sites other than the durable-audit branch.
IN-SCOPE FILES: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (classifier), cli/internal/agent/durable_audit_test.go (extend the transient table ~lines 175-185 via runAuditWithFinalizeErr).
title: execute-bead: classify SIGKILL/context-deadline durable-audit commit (signal: killed) + tracker-lock-timeout as transient, not operator-attention

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other languages (this project is Go-only at the check layer).
- Auto-run as recurring sweep (could be a future /schedule offer; not built into this bead).

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-215b08a2 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: F (Foundation): Operation pattern + IDGenerator + sentinels + ctx access: part 1

description:
PROBLEM
The ddx skill is the gateway most interactive agents read before deciding what to do. Without explicit mode guidance there, the agent can still interpret broad queue questions as implementation requests even after AGENTS.md and specs are updated.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps "What should I work on next?" directly to ddx bead ready, which is the worker-ready queue. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:1-16 defines doing work as draining the ready queue and recommends ddx work, but it does not say broad interactive sessions should manage intervention/planning instead of manually executing ready beads. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:43-49 tells agents to pick the next ready bead by default without distinguishing direct worker mode from broad interactive queue management.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the canonical ddx skill references and shipped copies so broad interactive DDx prompts enter queue_steward mode. The skill should tell agents to inspect intervention/focus status, summarize blockers, propose bead/spec changes, and suggest a worker when ready depth warrants it; explicit ddx work/try/execute-bead prompts remain bead_execution and may implement bead AC. Keep review prompts read-only unless the user asks to fix findings.

NON-SCOPE
- Editing SKILL.md frontmatter unless required by existing schema.
- Changing skill install/discovery mechanics.
- Implementing ddx work focus itself; this bead consumes that command surface.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: ddx-377ebf92 and ddx-3c6ca63f.
notes: SUPERSEDED by ddx-c2a66f45-wt-6bee7238. The interactive-steward routing bead now owns the first-stop interactive.md work, existing status/work/review reference cleanup, route fixtures, eval-driver schema update, and shipped-copy verification. Do not dispatch this bead independently.

description:
PROBLEM
  cli/internal/agent/types.go:441 sets DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000
  (3 hours). This is the per-attempt absolute wall-clock cap on a
  harness invocation. Two drain wedges observed in the 2026-05-09
  drain consumed the full 3 hours before timing out:

    ddx-c113a116: silent from 16:21 to 19:13:54 (3h0m0.018s),
                  then "do response complete 8472 tok in 3h0m0.018s,
                  0.8 tok/s" → timeout: context deadline exceeded.

    ddx-94a368b6: silent from 19:36 onward (drain killed before
                  natural timeout fired).

  3 hours is too long: a wedged worker burns operator time and budget
  before the timeout fires. 1 hour catches the same wedge class
  before they accumulate substantial cost.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/types.go:441
        DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
  - Comment at line 438: "Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout
    — so normal long tasks still complete while genuinely hung workers
    free themselves."
  - The 1.5x ratio against the 2h idle timeout (DefaultTimeoutMS at
    line 434) is preserved by halving both proportionally OR by
    halving wall-clock alone (idle timer protects against true silence
    independently).

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/types.go:441: change DefaultWallClockMS to
    3600000 (1 hour).
  - Update the comment at line 438 to reflect the 1h cap and explain
    the wedge-detection rationale (citing this bead).
  - DO NOT change DefaultTimeoutMS (idle timer); it remains at 2h,
    which now exceeds wall-clock — but that's fine, idle and
    wall-clock are separate triggers (whichever fires first wins).
    Document this in the comment.
  - Verify nothing in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:45-46 or
    cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:158-159 hardcodes the 3h value
    elsewhere; they should pull from DefaultWallClockMS.
  - Add a regression test: TestExecutor_WallClockCapsAtConfiguredBudget
    in cli/internal/agent/executor_test.go (or extend an existing
    test) that asserts ExecuteInDir cancels at the configured budget.

NON-SCOPE
  - Per-tool-call timeout (sister bead handles that as a structural
    prevention; this bead is the quick symptom guard).
  - Loop detection via stream-json watcher (separate concern).
  - Removing the underlying source of long Bash calls (sister beads
    ddx-1fda33d2, ddx-150758e7, ddx-f1c476c3 reduce per-push test
    cost; ddx-1f774edf removes per-bead pushes entirely).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent: per-tool-call wall-clock for
    Bash..." — structural prevention; this bead is the immediate
    symptom mitigation.
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf — once landed, drain stops pushing per
    bead, eliminating the 168s test tax that fuels wedges.
notes: Cancelled per user direction 2026-05-09: wall-clock cap is wrong policy. Reducing 3h to 1h would kill legitimately-hard-but-still-active work along with wedges. Right policy: detect 'no real progress' (loops + true idle) rather than capping absolute wall-clock. See sister bead ddx-1c3cad6f (reshaped to cover loop detection + meaningful-event idle reset).
title: fix(agent): reduce DefaultWallClockMS from 3h to 1h (faster wedge detection)

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-937de9fc is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.

   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree.
- TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.
- ddx doctor's two new lines ("Global Install" and "Project Install") render correctly.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
