description:
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead only adds the Niflheim observation as regression coverage and decision/audit wiring.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS

description:
PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1133 ExecuteBeadWorker.Run is 2,473 LOC — pre-loop setup (:1133-:1499) plus the entire iteration loop (for { at :1500). The loop body has comment-delimited phases that are natural extraction seams (candidate selection :1500-1799, wedge-guard+atomic-claim :1840-1958, intake :2012, lint gate :2452, dispatch+watchdog :2559-2741, post-attempt classification :2742+).

PROPOSED FIX: STRICTLY MECHANICAL hoist (no behavior change): extract the loop body into (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runIteration(ctx, loopState, ...) (iterationOutcome, error), leaving Run as setup + for { runIteration(...) }. Introduce a small loopState/iterationOutcome struct for the values currently closed over. The phase helper functions mostly already exist further down the file.

NON-SCOPE: do NOT also extract the dispatch/post-attempt sub-phases here (separate follow-up); do NOT touch ExecuteBeadWithConfig; no behavior/event/ordering change.
notes: Thrown out 2026-06-08 (operator decision via monitoring agent): code-health-only refactor (mechanical hoist of ExecuteBeadWorker.Run into runIteration, no behavior change). Repeatedly unfit for autonomous drain — fails on gpt-5.4-mini, loses ref-CAS races on land, leaks execute_bead_loop.go into main and wedges workers. Not load-bearing; re-file fresh if the god-function size ever actually bites. Stale preserve refs left in place but not to be rescued (would revert fizeau v0.14.48).

acceptance:
1. Go test `TestFederationForwardMutation_RoutesToOwner` proves a target node/project mutation is sent to exactly one owning spoke.
2. Go test `TestFederationForwardMutation_RejectsOfflineOrReadOnly` proves offline/stale/read_only spokes are refused without local fallback state.
3. Go test `TestFederationForwardMutation_IdempotentRequestID` proves retrying a request id returns the original result without repeating the remote command.
4. Go test `TestFederationForwardMutation_RejectsBroadcastTarget` proves a missing or multi-owner target is rejected.
5. `cd cli && go test -short ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/...` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Hub-mode needs to forward basic operator writes to exactly one owner node/project instead of remaining read-only or creating phantom local state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:150 specifies the owner-targeted write surface.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-007-federation-topology.md documents the 2026-06-08 amendment requiring owner-targeted basic writes.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:3 documents the current federation resolvers as read-only fan-out.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:23 defines FederationProvider with only Spokes and FanOut; there is no owner-targeted ForwardMutation path.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327 requires federated worker control and owner-targeted writes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a narrow federation forwarding interface and implementation for mutations that target one node/project/worker owner.
- Carry origin identity, forwarding path, request id/idempotency key, target node id, target project id, and expected version where supplied.
- Refuse offline, stale, read_only, missing-project, missing-owner, and broadcast-like requests with typed errors.
- Add idempotency storage or replay behavior for forwarded requests so retries do not duplicate workers/events/writes.
- Keep existing federated reads unchanged except for capability metadata needed by forwarding.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement write broadcast, distributed consensus, Axon backend, FEAT-029 managed-node outbound channels, or hub failover.
title: server: add owner-targeted federation mutation forwarding

acceptance:
1. ddx worker status --all-projects --json or the implemented equivalent shows helix, ddx, tablespec, pqueue, and heimq each with desired_count >= 1 and actual managed worker count >= 1, or each exception has a linked blocker bead.
2. Existing tmux/shell ddx work workers for those projects are stopped or explicitly classified as temporary external fallback with a blocker bead.
3. pgrep -af 'ddx work|claude|codex' is reviewed after migration and no retired attempt-owned ddx work, claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendant remains.
4. Each project has fresh worker progress evidence from the server-managed surface after migration.
5. Queue status for helix, ddx, tablespec, pqueue, and heimq is recorded, including projected blockers if burn-down within 24 hours is not realistic.
6. docs/helix/06-iterate/server-managed-workers-plan-2026-06-13.md is updated with final migration evidence.
7. make install is run after any ddx code change lands during migration.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The server-managed worker feature is not done until active dogfood queues stop depending on tmux/shell workers and are monitored through the server-managed surface. The operator specifically needs helix, ddx, tablespec, pqueue, and heimq to each have one worker steadily burning down its bead queue, with all in-flight queues expected to burn down in less than 24 hours unless blocked by explicit beads.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The plan's Migration Plan requires replacing external workers with server-managed desired state. Existing server start/stop at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3271-3285 and worker UI controls at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:12-42 were one-shot controls, so earlier dogfood used tmux windows and external ddx work processes. After the dependency beads land, the server should be authoritative for routine worker lifecycle.

PROPOSED FIX
Inventory active workers and queues, stop retired tmux/shell workers gracefully, verify no leftover attempt-owned ddx work/claude/codex descendants, set desired_count=1 for helix, ddx, tablespec, pqueue, and heimq via ddx worker/server API, and monitor through server APIs until queues drain or blockers are filed. Update the plan document with migration evidence and any exception beads.

NON-SCOPE
Do not force-kill unrelated interactive Claude/Codex/Codex sessions. Do not drain queues by manual implementation outside beads unless explicitly required to unstick a worker. Do not use tmux as the steady-state management mechanism.
title: ops: migrate active project queues to server-managed workers
